Input
{
  "description": "Analyze foreach optimizer patterns",
  "prompt": "Search for and analyze the _multi_tensor_* functions across all optimizer files to identify common patterns. Look for:\n\n1. Common structure/skeleton of these functions\n2. Repeated code patterns for:\n   - Empty params check\n   - Lr/beta conversion to scalar\n   - Device/dtype grouping\n   - Gradient maximize handling\n   - Weight decay application\n   - Parameter updates\n3. Files that could benefit from sharing code\n\nSearch in: /workspace/torch/optim/\n\nFiles to analyze: adadelta.py, adagrad.py, adam.py, adamax.py, asgd.py, nadam.py, radam.py, rmsprop.py, rprop.py, sgd.py\n\nReturn a summary of:\n- Common patterns found\n- Variations between optimizers\n- Recommendations for what to extract into _foreach.py",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "adfc5e13ecd836d90",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Now I have analyzed all 10 optimizer files. Let me compile a comprehensive report of my findings.\n\n## Summary: _multi_tensor_* Functions Analysis\n\nBased on my analysis of the `_multi_tensor_*` functions across all 10 optimizer files, here's a detailed breakdown:\n\n### Common Structural Skeleton\n\nAll `_multi_tensor_*` functions follow this general pattern:\n\n```python\ndef _multi_tensor_[OPTIMIZER](\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    # ... additional state tensors ...\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    # hyperparameters\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    # 1. Early returns & assertions\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n    \n    # 2. Capturable device checks\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(...)\n        assert all(p.device.type == step.device.type ...)\n    \n    # 3. Convert lr/betas to scalar\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n    \n    # 4. Group by device and dtype\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype([...])\n    \n    # 5. For each device/dtype group:\n    for (...), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        # Cast to proper types\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], ...)\n        \n        # Handle complex parameters\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(...)\n        \n        # Apply maximize, weight decay, step updates\n        # Algorithm-specific computations\n        # Final parameter update\n```\n\n### Common Patterns Found\n\n#### 1. **Empty Params Check**\nAll 10 optimizers check:\n```python\nif len(params) == 0:\n    return\n```\nThis is critical since foreach operations require non-empty lists.\n\n#### 2. **Lr/Beta Conversion to Scalar**\n```python\nlr = _to_scalar(lr)  # All use this\nbeta1 = _to_scalar(beta1)  # If applicable\n```\nEven adam.py (which has tensor betas) does scalar conversion first.\n\n#### 3. **Device/Dtype Grouping**\n```python\ngrouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n    [params, grads, square_avgs, ...]\n)\nfor (...), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n    device_params = cast(list[Tensor], ...)\n    # operations\n```\nAll 10 files use identical grouping logic - good reuse opportunity.\n\n#### 4. **Maximize Handling** (Common pattern: ~8/10 optimizers)\n```python\nif maximize:\n    device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n```\nAdadelta, Adagrad, Adam, Adamax, ASGD, NAdam, RAdam, RMSprop all use this.\n\n**Exception**: Rprop handles maximize differently with element-wise sign computations.\n\n#### 5. **Step Update (CPU Optimization)** (All 10 optimizers)\n```python\nif not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n    torch._foreach_add_(\n        grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n    )\nelse:\n    torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n```\nIdentical across all 10 files - highly duplicated code.\n\n#### 6. **Weight Decay Application** (9/10 optimizers)\n```python\nif weight_decay != 0:\n    if maximize:\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n    else:\n        device_grads = torch._foreach_add(\n            device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n        )\n```\nThis pattern appears in: Adadelta, Adagrad, Adam, Adamax, ASGD, NAdam, RAdam, RMSprop, SGD.\n\nRprop doesn't have weight decay parameter.\n\n#### 7. **Complex Parameter Handling**\n```python\nif has_complex:\n    _view_as_real(\n        device_params, device_grads, device_square_avgs, ...\n    )\n```\nPattern used in: Adadelta, Adagrad, Adam, Adamax, ASGD, NAdam, RAdam, RMSprop, Rprop (9/10).\n\nVariations in order/scope but same concept.\n\n#### 8. **Capturable Device Checks**\n```python\nif not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n    capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(supports_xla=False)\n    assert all(\n        p.device.type == step.device.type\n        and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n        for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n    )\n```\nAdadelta, Adamax, ASGD, NAdam, RAdam, RMSprop, Rprop all have variations of this.\n\n### Variations Between Optimizers\n\n#### Algorithm-Specific Variations:\n\n1. **Adadelta**: Complex exponential moving averages\n2. **Adagrad**: Per-parameter adaptive learning rates + sparse gradient handling\n3. **Adam**: Bias correction, amsgrad option, beta handling\n4. **Adamax**: Infinity norm instead of squared gradients\n5. **ASGD**: Averaging mechanism, eta/mu updates\n6. **NAdam**: Nesterov momentum + momentum decay calculations\n7. **RAdam**: SMA variance rectification (rho_t calculation)\n8. **RMSprop**: Centered variant with grad averaging\n9. **Rprop**: Sign-based updates, no gradients in certain conditions\n10. **SGD**: Momentum/Nesterov, simplest algorithm\n\n### Code Duplication Analysis\n\nHigh duplication candidates (repeated verbatim in multiple files):\n\n| Pattern | Files | Lines | Priority |\n|---------|-------|-------|----------|\n| Empty params check | 10/10 | 2-3 | HIGH |\n| Step update (CPU opt) | 10/10 | 4-7 | HIGH |\n| Device/dtype grouping | 10/10 | 3-4 | HIGH |\n| Capturable checks | 7/10 | 6-9 | HIGH |\n| Maximize negation | 8/10 | 1-2 | HIGH |\n| Weight decay pattern | 9/10 | 5-6 | HIGH |\n| Complex view_as_real | 9/10 | 2-4 | MEDIUM |\n| Differentiable assertion | 10/10 | 1 | HIGH |\n\n### Recommendations for _foreach.py Extraction\n\n**Extract as utility functions:**\n\n1. **`_check_empty_params(params)`** - 2 lines\n   - Used in: All 10 optimizers\n\n2. **`_handle_step_updates(device_state_steps, is_cpu)`** - 7 lines\n   - Used in: All 10 optimizers\n   - Identical CPU optimization logic\n\n3. **`_apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)`** - 2 lines\n   - Used in: 8 optimizers (Adadelta, Adagrad, Adam, Adamax, ASGD, NAdam, RAdam, RMSprop)\n\n4. **`_apply_weight_decay(device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize)`** - 6 lines\n   - Used in: 9 optimizers (all except Rprop)\n   - Includes memory reuse logic for maximize case\n\n5. **`_handle_capturable_devices(params, state_steps, capturable, supports_xla=False)`** - 9 lines\n   - Used in: 7 optimizers\n   - Extraction would reduce duplication significantly\n\n6. **`_view_as_real_for_complex(has_complex, *tensors)`** - 2 lines\n   - Used in: 9 optimizers\n   - Wrapper around `_view_as_real`\n\n7. **`_get_grouped_tensors_by_device_dtype(tensor_lists)`** - 2-3 lines\n   - Used in: All 10 optimizers\n   - Extracted helper could improve readability\n\n**Optimization Impact:**\n- Estimated savings: 15-20 lines per optimizer on average\n- Total codebase reduction: ~150-200 lines\n- Improved maintainability: Critical changes need updates in one place only\n\n### Files Most Suitable for Refactoring\n\n1. **Adam & Adamax** - Similar structure, both have beta handling\n2. **ASGD, NAdam, RAdam** - Each has unique complexity, moderate duplication\n3. **RMSprop** - Has centered variant complexity, good refactoring candidate\n4. **Adadelta** - Moderate complexity, high duplication\n\n### Key Observations\n\n- **No single `.py` extract strategy fits all**: Some optimizers (Rprop) differ significantly\n- **Conditional imports recommended**: Use helper functions with optional parameters\n- **Memory reuse pattern**: Weight decay + maximize reuses `device_grads` - worth documenting\n- **CPU tensor optimization**: Step update pattern is critical for performance - keep close to implementation\n- **Capturable state**: Different optimizers have different state tensor combinations - requires templating\n\nWould you like me to create a detailed refactoring proposal for specific helper functions?",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Search for and analyze the _multi_tensor_* functions across all optimizer files to identify common patterns. Look for:\n\n1. Common structure/skeleton of these functions\n2. Repeated code patterns for:\n   - Empty params check\n   - Lr/beta conversion to scalar\n   - Device/dtype grouping\n   - Gradient maximize handling\n   - Weight decay application\n   - Parameter updates\n3. Files that could benefit from sharing code\n\nSearch in: /workspace/torch/optim/\n\nFiles to analyze: adadelta.py, adagrad.py, adam.py, adamax.py, asgd.py, nadam.py, radam.py, rmsprop.py, rprop.py, sgd.py\n\nReturn a summary of:\n- Common patterns found\n- Variations between optimizers\n- Recommendations for what to extract into _foreach.py",

Input

Output
"     1\u2192# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\n     2\u2192from typing import Any, cast, Optional, Union\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192import torch\n     5\u2192from torch import Tensor\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192from .optimizer import (\n     8\u2192    _capturable_doc,\n     9\u2192    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    10\u2192    _differentiable_doc,\n    11\u2192    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    12\u2192    _foreach_doc,\n    13\u2192    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    14\u2192    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    15\u2192    _maximize_doc,\n    16\u2192    _params_doc,\n    17\u2192    _to_scalar,\n    18\u2192    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    19\u2192    _view_as_real,\n    20\u2192    Optimizer,\n    21\u2192    ParamsT,\n    22\u2192)\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192__all__ = [\"Adadelta\", \"adadelta\"]\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192class Adadelta(Optimizer):\n    29\u2192    def __init__(\n    30\u2192        self,\n    31\u2192        params: ParamsT,\n    32\u2192        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1.0,\n    33\u2192        rho: float = 0.9,\n    34\u2192        eps: float = 1e-6,\n    35\u2192        weight_decay: float = 0,\n    36\u2192        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    37\u2192        *,\n    38\u2192        capturable: bool = False,\n    39\u2192        maximize: bool = False,\n    40\u2192        differentiable: bool = False,\n    41\u2192    ):\n    42\u2192        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n    43\u2192            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n    44\u2192        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n    45\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n    46\u2192        if not 0.0 <= rho <= 1.0:\n    47\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid rho value: {rho}\")\n    48\u2192        if not 0.0 <= eps:\n    49\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid epsilon value: {eps}\")\n    50\u2192        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n    51\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192        defaults = {\n    54\u2192            \"lr\": lr,\n    55\u2192            \"rho\": rho,\n    56\u2192            \"eps\": eps,\n    57\u2192            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n    58\u2192            \"maximize\": maximize,\n    59\u2192            \"capturable\": capturable,\n    60\u2192            \"foreach\": foreach,\n    61\u2192            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n    62\u2192        }\n    63\u2192        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192    def __setstate__(self, state):\n    66\u2192        super().__setstate__(state)\n    67\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n    68\u2192            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n    69\u2192            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n    70\u2192            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n    71\u2192            group.setdefault(\"capturable\", False)\n    72\u2192            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n    73\u2192                p_state = self.state.get(p, [])\n    74\u2192                if len(p_state) != 0 and not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n    75\u2192                    step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n    76\u2192                    p_state[\"step\"] = (\n    77\u2192                        torch.tensor(\n    78\u2192                            step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n    79\u2192                        )\n    80\u2192                        if group[\"capturable\"]\n    81\u2192                        else torch.tensor(step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n    82\u2192                    )\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192    def _init_group(\n    85\u2192        self,\n    86\u2192        group: dict[str, Any],\n    87\u2192        params_with_grad: list[Tensor],\n    88\u2192        grads: list[Tensor],\n    89\u2192        square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    90\u2192        acc_deltas: list[Tensor],\n    91\u2192        state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    92\u2192    ):\n    93\u2192        has_complex = False\n    94\u2192        p: Tensor\n    95\u2192        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n    96\u2192            if p.grad is None:\n    97\u2192                continue\n    98\u2192            has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n    99\u2192            params_with_grad.append(p)\n   100\u2192            if p.grad.is_sparse:\n   101\u2192                raise RuntimeError(\"Adadelta does not support sparse gradients\")\n   102\u2192            grads.append(p.grad)\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192            state = self.state[p]\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192            # Lazy state initialization\n   107\u2192            if len(state) == 0:\n   108\u2192                state[\"step\"] = (\n   109\u2192                    torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n   110\u2192                    if group[\"capturable\"]\n   111\u2192                    else torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   112\u2192                )\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192                state[\"square_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   115\u2192                    p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   116\u2192                )\n   117\u2192                state[\"acc_delta\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   118\u2192                    p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   119\u2192                )\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192            square_avgs.append(state[\"square_avg\"])\n   122\u2192            acc_deltas.append(state[\"acc_delta\"])\n   123\u2192            state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192        return has_complex\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n   128\u2192    def step(self, closure=None):\n   129\u2192        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192        Args:\n   132\u2192            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n   133\u2192                and returns the loss.\n   134\u2192        \"\"\"\n   135\u2192        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192        loss = None\n   138\u2192        if closure is not None:\n   139\u2192            with torch.enable_grad():\n   140\u2192                loss = closure()\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n   143\u2192            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n   144\u2192            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n   145\u2192            square_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n   146\u2192            acc_deltas: list[Tensor] = []\n   147\u2192            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n   148\u2192            (\n   149\u2192                lr,\n   150\u2192                rho,\n   151\u2192                eps,\n   152\u2192                weight_decay,\n   153\u2192                foreach,\n   154\u2192                maximize,\n   155\u2192                differentiable,\n   156\u2192                capturable,\n   157\u2192            ) = (\n   158\u2192                group[\"lr\"],\n   159\u2192                group[\"rho\"],\n   160\u2192                group[\"eps\"],\n   161\u2192                group[\"weight_decay\"],\n   162\u2192                group[\"foreach\"],\n   163\u2192                group[\"maximize\"],\n   164\u2192                group[\"differentiable\"],\n   165\u2192                group[\"capturable\"],\n   166\u2192            )\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192            has_complex = self._init_group(\n   169\u2192                group, params_with_grad, grads, square_avgs, acc_deltas, state_steps\n   170\u2192            )\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192            adadelta(\n   173\u2192                params_with_grad,\n   174\u2192                grads,\n   175\u2192                square_avgs,\n   176\u2192                acc_deltas,\n   177\u2192                state_steps,\n   178\u2192                lr=lr,\n   179\u2192                rho=rho,\n   180\u2192                eps=eps,\n   181\u2192                weight_decay=weight_decay,\n   182\u2192                foreach=foreach,\n   183\u2192                maximize=maximize,\n   184\u2192                differentiable=differentiable,\n   185\u2192                capturable=capturable,\n   186\u2192                has_complex=has_complex,\n   187\u2192            )\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192        return loss\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192Adadelta.__doc__ = (\n   193\u2192    r\"\"\"Implements Adadelta algorithm.\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192    .. math::\n   196\u2192       \\begin{aligned}\n   197\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   198\u2192            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)},\n   199\u2192                \\: f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\rho \\text{ (decay)},\n   200\u2192                \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)}                                                \\\\\n   201\u2192            &\\textbf{initialize} :  v_0  \\leftarrow 0 \\: \\text{ (square avg)},\n   202\u2192                \\: u_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\: \\text{ (accumulate variables)}                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   203\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   204\u2192            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   205\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n   206\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                                    \\\\\n   207\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n   208\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm} v_t      \\leftarrow v_{t-1} \\rho + g^2_t (1 - \\rho)                    \\\\\n   209\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}\\Delta x_t    \\leftarrow   \\frac{\\sqrt{u_{t-1} +\n   210\u2192                \\epsilon }}{ \\sqrt{v_t + \\epsilon}  }g_t \\hspace{21mm}                           \\\\\n   211\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm} u_t  \\leftarrow   u_{t-1}  \\rho +\n   212\u2192                 \\Delta x^2_t  (1 - \\rho)                                                        \\\\\n   213\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t      \\leftarrow   \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma  \\Delta x_t            \\\\\n   214\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   215\u2192            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   216\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   217\u2192       \\end{aligned}\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `ADADELTA: An Adaptive Learning Rate Method`_.\n   220\u2192    \"\"\"\n   221\u2192    + rf\"\"\"\n   222\u2192    Args:\n   223\u2192        {_params_doc}\n   224\u2192        lr (float, Tensor, optional): coefficient that scale delta before it is applied\n   225\u2192            to the parameters (default: 1.0)\n   226\u2192        rho (float, optional): coefficient used for computing a running average\n   227\u2192            of squared gradients (default: 0.9). A higher value of `rho` will\n   228\u2192            result in a slower average, which can be helpful for preventing\n   229\u2192            oscillations in the learning process.\n   230\u2192        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   231\u2192            numerical stability (default: 1e-6).\n   232\u2192        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   233\u2192        {_foreach_doc}\n   234\u2192        {_capturable_doc}\n   235\u2192        {_maximize_doc}\n   236\u2192        {_differentiable_doc}\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192    .. _ADADELTA\\: An Adaptive Learning Rate Method:\n   239\u2192        https://arxiv.org/abs/1212.5701\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192    \"\"\"\n   242\u2192)\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192def _single_tensor_adadelta(\n   246\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   247\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   248\u2192    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   249\u2192    acc_deltas: list[Tensor],\n   250\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   251\u2192    *,\n   252\u2192    lr: float,\n   253\u2192    rho: float,\n   254\u2192    eps: float,\n   255\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   256\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   257\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   258\u2192    capturable: bool,\n   259\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   260\u2192):\n   261\u2192    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   262\u2192    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   263\u2192        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n   264\u2192            supports_xla=False\n   265\u2192        )\n   266\u2192        assert all(\n   267\u2192            p.device.type == step.device.type\n   268\u2192            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   269\u2192            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n   270\u2192        ), (\n   271\u2192            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   272\u2192        )\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   275\u2192        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192    for param, grad, square_avg, acc_delta, step in zip(\n   278\u2192        params, grads, square_avgs, acc_deltas, state_steps\n   279\u2192    ):\n   280\u2192        step += 1\n   281\u2192        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192        if weight_decay != 0:\n   284\u2192            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   285\u2192\n   286\u2192        if torch.is_complex(param):\n   287\u2192            square_avg = torch.view_as_real(square_avg)\n   288\u2192            acc_delta = torch.view_as_real(acc_delta)\n   289\u2192            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192        square_avg.mul_(rho).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - rho)\n   292\u2192        std = square_avg.add(eps).sqrt_()\n   293\u2192        delta = acc_delta.add(eps).sqrt_()\n   294\u2192        if differentiable:\n   295\u2192            delta = delta.clone()\n   296\u2192        delta.div_(std).mul_(grad)\n   297\u2192        acc_delta.mul_(rho).addcmul_(delta, delta, value=1 - rho)\n   298\u2192\n   299\u2192        if torch.is_complex(param):\n   300\u2192            delta = torch.view_as_complex(delta)\n   301\u2192        param.add_(delta, alpha=-lr)\n   302\u2192\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192def _multi_tensor_adadelta(\n   305\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   306\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   307\u2192    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   308\u2192    acc_deltas: list[Tensor],\n   309\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   310\u2192    *,\n   311\u2192    lr: float,\n   312\u2192    rho: float,\n   313\u2192    eps: float,\n   314\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   315\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   316\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   317\u2192    capturable: bool,\n   318\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   319\u2192):\n   320\u2192    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   323\u2192    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   324\u2192        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n   325\u2192            supports_xla=False\n   326\u2192        )\n   327\u2192        assert all(\n   328\u2192            p.device.type == step.device.type\n   329\u2192            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   330\u2192            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n   331\u2192        ), (\n   332\u2192            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   333\u2192        )\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192    if len(params) == 0:\n   336\u2192        return\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   341\u2192        [params, grads, square_avgs, acc_deltas, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   342\u2192    )\n   343\u2192    for (\n   344\u2192        device_params_,\n   345\u2192        device_grads_,\n   346\u2192        device_square_avgs_,\n   347\u2192        device_acc_deltas_,\n   348\u2192        device_state_steps_,\n   349\u2192    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n   350\u2192        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n   351\u2192        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n   352\u2192        device_square_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], device_square_avgs_)\n   353\u2192        device_acc_deltas = cast(list[Tensor], device_acc_deltas_)\n   354\u2192        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n   355\u2192        if has_complex:\n   356\u2192            _view_as_real(\n   357\u2192                device_params, device_grads, device_square_avgs, device_acc_deltas\n   358\u2192            )\n   359\u2192\n   360\u2192        # Update steps\n   361\u2192        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   362\u2192        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   363\u2192        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   364\u2192        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   365\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(\n   366\u2192                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   367\u2192            )\n   368\u2192        else:\n   369\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n   370\u2192\n   371\u2192        if maximize:\n   372\u2192            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   373\u2192\n   374\u2192        if weight_decay != 0:\n   375\u2192            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n   376\u2192            if maximize:\n   377\u2192                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n   378\u2192            else:\n   379\u2192                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   380\u2192                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   381\u2192                )\n   382\u2192\n   383\u2192        torch._foreach_mul_(device_square_avgs, rho)\n   384\u2192        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n   385\u2192            device_square_avgs, device_grads, device_grads, value=1 - rho\n   386\u2192        )\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192        std = torch._foreach_add(device_square_avgs, eps)\n   389\u2192        torch._foreach_sqrt_(std)\n   390\u2192\n   391\u2192        deltas = torch._foreach_add(device_acc_deltas, eps)\n   392\u2192        torch._foreach_sqrt_(deltas)\n   393\u2192        torch._foreach_div_(deltas, std)\n   394\u2192        torch._foreach_mul_(deltas, device_grads)\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192        torch._foreach_mul_(device_acc_deltas, rho)\n   397\u2192        torch._foreach_addcmul_(device_acc_deltas, deltas, deltas, value=1 - rho)\n   398\u2192\n   399\u2192        # If LR is a tensor, the else branch will internally call item()\n   400\u2192        # which will cause silent incorrectness if we are capturing\n   401\u2192        if capturable and isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor):\n   402\u2192            torch._foreach_mul_(deltas, -lr)\n   403\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(device_params, deltas)\n   404\u2192        else:\n   405\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(device_params, deltas, alpha=-lr)\n   406\u2192\n   407\u2192\n   408\u2192@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_adadelta)\n   409\u2192def adadelta(\n   410\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   411\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   412\u2192    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   413\u2192    acc_deltas: list[Tensor],\n   414\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   415\u2192    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n   416\u2192    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n   417\u2192    capturable: bool = False,\n   418\u2192    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n   419\u2192    differentiable: bool = False,\n   420\u2192    has_complex: bool = False,\n   421\u2192    *,\n   422\u2192    lr: float,\n   423\u2192    rho: float,\n   424\u2192    eps: float,\n   425\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   426\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   427\u2192):\n   428\u2192    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs Adadelta algorithm computation.\n   429\u2192\n   430\u2192    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adadelta` for details.\n   431\u2192    \"\"\"\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192    # this check is slow during compilation, so we skip it\n   434\u2192    # if it's strictly needed we can add this check back in dynamo\n   435\u2192    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and not all(\n   436\u2192        isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps\n   437\u2192    ):\n   438\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\n   439\u2192            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n   440\u2192        )\n   441\u2192\n   442\u2192    # We still respect when the user inputs False for foreach.\n   443\u2192    if foreach is None:\n   444\u2192        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n   445\u2192            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n   446\u2192        )\n   447\u2192\n   448\u2192    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   449\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n   450\u2192\n   451\u2192    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   452\u2192        func = _multi_tensor_adadelta\n   453\u2192    else:\n   454\u2192        func = _single_tensor_adadelta\n   455\u2192\n   456\u2192    func(\n   457\u2192        params,\n   458\u2192        grads,\n   459\u2192        square_avgs,\n   460\u2192        acc_deltas,\n   461\u2192        state_steps,\n   462\u2192        lr=lr,\n   463\u2192        rho=rho,\n   464\u2192        eps=eps,\n   465\u2192        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n   466\u2192        maximize=maximize,\n   467\u2192        differentiable=differentiable,\n   468\u2192        capturable=capturable,\n   469\u2192        has_complex=has_complex,\n   470\u2192    )\n   471\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
15. Read @ -
Input

  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py"

Output
"     1\u2192# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\n     2\u2192from typing import cast, Optional, Union\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192import torch\n     5\u2192from torch import Tensor\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192from .optimizer import (\n     8\u2192    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n     9\u2192    _device_dtype_check_for_fused,\n    10\u2192    _differentiable_doc,\n    11\u2192    _foreach_doc,\n    12\u2192    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    13\u2192    _get_value,\n    14\u2192    _maximize_doc,\n    15\u2192    _params_doc,\n    16\u2192    _to_scalar,\n    17\u2192    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    18\u2192    _view_as_real,\n    19\u2192    Optimizer,\n    20\u2192    ParamsT,\n    21\u2192)\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192__all__ = [\"Adagrad\", \"adagrad\"]\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192class Adagrad(Optimizer):\n    28\u2192    def __init__(\n    29\u2192        self,\n    30\u2192        params: ParamsT,\n    31\u2192        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-2,\n    32\u2192        lr_decay: float = 0,\n    33\u2192        weight_decay: float = 0,\n    34\u2192        initial_accumulator_value: float = 0,\n    35\u2192        eps: float = 1e-10,\n    36\u2192        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    37\u2192        *,\n    38\u2192        maximize: bool = False,\n    39\u2192        differentiable: bool = False,\n    40\u2192        fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    41\u2192    ):\n    42\u2192        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n    43\u2192            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n    44\u2192        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n    45\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n    46\u2192        if not 0.0 <= lr_decay:\n    47\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid lr_decay value: {lr_decay}\")\n    48\u2192        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n    49\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n    50\u2192        if not 0.0 <= initial_accumulator_value:\n    51\u2192            raise ValueError(\n    52\u2192                f\"Invalid initial_accumulator_value value: {initial_accumulator_value}\"\n    53\u2192            )\n    54\u2192        if not 0.0 <= eps:\n    55\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid epsilon value: {eps}\")\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192        defaults = {\n    58\u2192            \"lr\": lr,\n    59\u2192            \"lr_decay\": lr_decay,\n    60\u2192            \"eps\": eps,\n    61\u2192            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n    62\u2192            \"initial_accumulator_value\": initial_accumulator_value,\n    63\u2192            \"foreach\": foreach,\n    64\u2192            \"maximize\": maximize,\n    65\u2192            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n    66\u2192            \"fused\": fused,\n    67\u2192        }\n    68\u2192        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192        if fused:\n    71\u2192            if differentiable:\n    72\u2192                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support `differentiable`\")\n    73\u2192            if foreach:\n    74\u2192                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` and `foreach` cannot be `True` together.\")\n    75\u2192            self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = True\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n    78\u2192            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n    79\u2192                state = self.state[p]\n    80\u2192                state[\"step\"] = (\n    81\u2192                    torch.zeros(\n    82\u2192                        (),\n    83\u2192                        dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(is_fused=group[\"fused\"]),\n    84\u2192                        device=p.device,\n    85\u2192                    )\n    86\u2192                    if group[\"fused\"]\n    87\u2192                    else torch.tensor(0.0, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n    88\u2192                )\n    89\u2192                init_value = (\n    90\u2192                    complex(initial_accumulator_value, initial_accumulator_value)\n    91\u2192                    if torch.is_complex(p)\n    92\u2192                    else initial_accumulator_value\n    93\u2192                )\n    94\u2192                state[\"sum\"] = torch.full_like(\n    95\u2192                    p, init_value, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n    96\u2192                )\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192    def __setstate__(self, state):\n    99\u2192        super().__setstate__(state)\n   100\u2192        #  define \"fused\" for\n   101\u2192        #  MYPY error: Name \"fused\" may be undefined\n   102\u2192        fused = None\n   103\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n   104\u2192            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n   105\u2192            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n   106\u2192            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n   107\u2192            fused = group.setdefault(\"fused\", None)\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192        state_values = list(self.state.values())\n   110\u2192        step_is_tensor = (len(state_values) != 0) and torch.is_tensor(\n   111\u2192            state_values[0][\"step\"]\n   112\u2192        )\n   113\u2192        if not step_is_tensor:\n   114\u2192            for s in state_values:\n   115\u2192                s[\"step\"] = torch.tensor(\n   116\u2192                    float(s[\"step\"]), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(is_fused=fused)\n   117\u2192                )\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192    def share_memory(self):\n   120\u2192        \"\"\"Calls tensor.share_memory_() on the state sum tensors.\"\"\"\n   121\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n   122\u2192            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n   123\u2192                state = self.state[p]\n   124\u2192                state[\"sum\"].share_memory_()\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192    def _init_group(self, group, params_with_grad, grads, state_sums, state_steps):\n   127\u2192        has_sparse_grad, has_complex = False, False\n   128\u2192        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n   129\u2192            if p.grad is not None:\n   130\u2192                if group[\"fused\"] and getattr(\n   131\u2192                    self,\n   132\u2192                    \"_need_device_dtype_check_for_fused\",\n   133\u2192                    True,\n   134\u2192                ):\n   135\u2192                    _device_dtype_check_for_fused(p, cuda_unsupported=True)\n   136\u2192                    self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = False\n   137\u2192                has_sparse_grad |= p.grad.is_sparse\n   138\u2192                has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n   139\u2192                params_with_grad.append(p)\n   140\u2192                grads.append(p.grad)\n   141\u2192                state = self.state[p]\n   142\u2192                state_sums.append(state[\"sum\"])\n   143\u2192                state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192        return has_sparse_grad, has_complex\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n   148\u2192    def step(self, closure=None):\n   149\u2192        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192        Args:\n   152\u2192            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n   153\u2192                and returns the loss.\n   154\u2192        \"\"\"\n   155\u2192        loss = None\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192        if closure is not None:\n   158\u2192            with torch.enable_grad():\n   159\u2192                loss = closure()\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n   162\u2192            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n   163\u2192            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n   164\u2192            state_sums: list[Tensor] = []\n   165\u2192            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192            has_sparse_grad, has_complex = self._init_group(\n   168\u2192                group, params_with_grad, grads, state_sums, state_steps\n   169\u2192            )\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192            adagrad(\n   172\u2192                params_with_grad,\n   173\u2192                grads,\n   174\u2192                state_sums,\n   175\u2192                state_steps,\n   176\u2192                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n   177\u2192                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n   178\u2192                lr_decay=group[\"lr_decay\"],\n   179\u2192                eps=group[\"eps\"],\n   180\u2192                has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n   181\u2192                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n   182\u2192                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n   183\u2192                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n   184\u2192                has_complex=has_complex,\n   185\u2192                fused=group[\"fused\"],\n   186\u2192                grad_scale=getattr(self, \"grad_scale\", None),\n   187\u2192                found_inf=getattr(self, \"found_inf\", None),\n   188\u2192            )\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192        return loss\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192Adagrad.__doc__ = (\n   194\u2192    r\"\"\"Implements Adagrad algorithm.\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192    .. math::\n   197\u2192       \\begin{aligned}\n   198\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   199\u2192            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n   200\u2192                \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\n   201\u2192            &\\hspace{12mm}    \\tau \\text{ (initial accumulator value)}, \\: \\eta\\text{ (lr decay)}\\\\\n   202\u2192            &\\textbf{initialize} :  state\\_sum_0 \\leftarrow \\tau                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   203\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   204\u2192            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   205\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n   206\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm} \\tilde{\\gamma}    \\leftarrow \\gamma / (1 +(t-1) \\eta)                  \\\\\n   207\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm} \\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                          \\\\\n   208\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda \\theta_{t-1}                             \\\\\n   209\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}state\\_sum_t  \\leftarrow  state\\_sum_{t-1} + g^2_t                      \\\\\n   210\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow\n   211\u2192                \\theta_{t-1}- \\tilde{\\gamma} \\frac{g_t}{\\sqrt{state\\_sum_t}+\\epsilon}            \\\\\n   212\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   213\u2192            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   214\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   215\u2192       \\end{aligned}\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning\n   218\u2192    and Stochastic Optimization`_.\n   219\u2192    \"\"\"\n   220\u2192    + rf\"\"\"\n   221\u2192    Args:\n   222\u2192        {_params_doc}\n   223\u2192        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n   224\u2192        lr_decay (float, optional): learning rate decay (default: 0)\n   225\u2192        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   226\u2192        initial_accumulator_value (float, optional): initial value of the\n   227\u2192            sum of squares of gradients (default: 0)\n   228\u2192        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   229\u2192            numerical stability (default: 1e-10)\n   230\u2192        {_foreach_doc}\n   231\u2192        {_maximize_doc}\n   232\u2192        {_differentiable_doc}\n   233\u2192        fused (bool, optional): whether the fused implementation (CPU only) is used.\n   234\u2192            Currently, `torch.float64`, `torch.float32`, `torch.float16`, and `torch.bfloat16`\n   235\u2192            are supported. (default: None). Please note that the fused implementations does not\n   236\u2192            support sparse or complex gradients.\n   237\u2192    .. _Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning and Stochastic\n   238\u2192        Optimization: http://jmlr.org/papers/v12/duchi11a.html\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192    \"\"\"\n   241\u2192)\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192def adagrad(\n   245\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   246\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   247\u2192    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n   248\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   249\u2192    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n   250\u2192    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n   251\u2192    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n   252\u2192    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n   253\u2192    # setting these as kwargs for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n   254\u2192    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n   255\u2192    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n   256\u2192    differentiable: bool = False,\n   257\u2192    has_complex: bool = False,\n   258\u2192    *,\n   259\u2192    lr: float,\n   260\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   261\u2192    lr_decay: float,\n   262\u2192    eps: float,\n   263\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   264\u2192):\n   265\u2192    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs Adagrad algorithm computation.\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adagrad` for details.\n   268\u2192    \"\"\"\n   269\u2192    if not all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps):\n   270\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\n   271\u2192            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n   272\u2192        )\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\n   275\u2192    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\n   276\u2192    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\n   277\u2192    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\n   278\u2192    if fused is None and foreach is None:\n   279\u2192        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n   280\u2192            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n   281\u2192        )\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192    if fused is None:\n   284\u2192        fused = False\n   285\u2192    if foreach is None:\n   286\u2192        foreach = False\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   289\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n   290\u2192    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   291\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\")\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192    if fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   294\u2192        func = _fused_adagrad\n   295\u2192    elif foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   296\u2192        func = _multi_tensor_adagrad\n   297\u2192    else:\n   298\u2192        func = _single_tensor_adagrad\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192    func(\n   301\u2192        params,\n   302\u2192        grads,\n   303\u2192        state_sums,\n   304\u2192        state_steps,\n   305\u2192        lr=lr,\n   306\u2192        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n   307\u2192        lr_decay=lr_decay,\n   308\u2192        eps=eps,\n   309\u2192        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n   310\u2192        maximize=maximize,\n   311\u2192        differentiable=differentiable,\n   312\u2192        has_complex=has_complex,\n   313\u2192        grad_scale=grad_scale,\n   314\u2192        found_inf=found_inf,\n   315\u2192    )\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192\n   318\u2192def _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, values):\n   319\u2192    size = grad.size()\n   320\u2192    return torch.sparse_coo_tensor(grad_indices, values, size)\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192\n   323\u2192def _single_tensor_adagrad(\n   324\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   325\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   326\u2192    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n   327\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   328\u2192    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n   329\u2192    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n   330\u2192    *,\n   331\u2192    lr: float,\n   332\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   333\u2192    lr_decay: float,\n   334\u2192    eps: float,\n   335\u2192    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n   336\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   337\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   338\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   339\u2192):\n   340\u2192    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   343\u2192        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192    for param, grad, state_sum, step_t in zip(params, grads, state_sums, state_steps):\n   346\u2192        # update step\n   347\u2192        step_t += 1\n   348\u2192        step = _get_value(step_t)\n   349\u2192        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n   350\u2192\n   351\u2192        if weight_decay != 0:\n   352\u2192            if grad.is_sparse:\n   353\u2192                raise RuntimeError(\n   354\u2192                    \"weight_decay option is not compatible with sparse gradients\"\n   355\u2192                )\n   356\u2192            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192        clr = lr / (1 + (step - 1) * lr_decay)\n   359\u2192\n   360\u2192        if grad.is_sparse:\n   361\u2192            grad = grad.coalesce()  # the update is non-linear so indices must be unique\n   362\u2192            grad_indices = grad._indices()\n   363\u2192            grad_values = grad._values()\n   364\u2192\n   365\u2192            state_sum.add_(_make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values.pow(2)))\n   366\u2192            std = state_sum.sparse_mask(grad)\n   367\u2192            std_values = std._values().sqrt_().add_(eps)\n   368\u2192            param.add_(\n   369\u2192                _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values / std_values), alpha=-clr\n   370\u2192            )\n   371\u2192        else:\n   372\u2192            is_complex = torch.is_complex(param)\n   373\u2192            if is_complex:\n   374\u2192                grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   375\u2192                state_sum = torch.view_as_real(state_sum)\n   376\u2192                param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   377\u2192            state_sum.addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1)\n   378\u2192            if differentiable:\n   379\u2192                std = state_sum.sqrt() + eps\n   380\u2192            else:\n   381\u2192                std = state_sum.sqrt().add_(eps)\n   382\u2192            param.addcdiv_(grad, std, value=-clr)\n   383\u2192            if is_complex:\n   384\u2192                param = torch.view_as_complex(param)\n   385\u2192                state_sum = torch.view_as_complex(state_sum)\n   386\u2192\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192def _multi_tensor_adagrad(\n   389\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   390\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   391\u2192    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n   392\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   393\u2192    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n   394\u2192    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n   395\u2192    *,\n   396\u2192    lr: float,\n   397\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   398\u2192    lr_decay: float,\n   399\u2192    eps: float,\n   400\u2192    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n   401\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   402\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   403\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   404\u2192):\n   405\u2192    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   406\u2192    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n   407\u2192\n   408\u2192    # Foreach functions will throw errors if given empty lists\n   409\u2192    if len(params) == 0:\n   410\u2192        return\n   411\u2192\n   412\u2192    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   413\u2192\n   414\u2192    grouped_tensorlists = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   415\u2192        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   416\u2192    )\n   417\u2192    for (\n   418\u2192        device_params_,\n   419\u2192        device_grads_,\n   420\u2192        device_state_sums_,\n   421\u2192        device_state_steps_,\n   422\u2192    ), _ in grouped_tensorlists.values():\n   423\u2192        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n   424\u2192        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n   425\u2192        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n   426\u2192        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n   429\u2192            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n   430\u2192        )\n   431\u2192\n   432\u2192        if device_has_sparse_grad:\n   433\u2192            _single_tensor_adagrad(\n   434\u2192                device_params,\n   435\u2192                device_grads,\n   436\u2192                device_state_sums,\n   437\u2192                device_state_steps,\n   438\u2192                lr=lr,\n   439\u2192                weight_decay=weight_decay,\n   440\u2192                lr_decay=lr_decay,\n   441\u2192                eps=eps,\n   442\u2192                has_sparse_grad=True,\n   443\u2192                maximize=maximize,\n   444\u2192                differentiable=differentiable,\n   445\u2192                has_complex=has_complex,\n   446\u2192                grad_scale=grad_scale,\n   447\u2192                found_inf=found_inf,\n   448\u2192            )\n   449\u2192            continue\n   450\u2192\n   451\u2192        # Handle complex parameters\n   452\u2192        if has_complex:\n   453\u2192            _view_as_real(device_params, device_grads, device_state_sums)\n   454\u2192\n   455\u2192        if maximize:\n   456\u2192            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   457\u2192\n   458\u2192        # Update steps\n   459\u2192        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   460\u2192        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   461\u2192        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   462\u2192        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   463\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(\n   464\u2192                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   465\u2192            )\n   466\u2192        else:\n   467\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n   468\u2192\n   469\u2192        if weight_decay != 0:\n   470\u2192            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n   471\u2192            if maximize:\n   472\u2192                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n   473\u2192            else:\n   474\u2192                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   475\u2192                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   476\u2192                )\n   477\u2192\n   478\u2192        minus_clr = [\n   479\u2192            -lr / (1 + (_get_value(step) - 1) * lr_decay) for step in device_state_steps\n   480\u2192        ]\n   481\u2192\n   482\u2192        torch._foreach_addcmul_(device_state_sums, device_grads, device_grads, value=1)\n   483\u2192\n   484\u2192        std = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_state_sums)\n   485\u2192        torch._foreach_add_(std, eps)\n   486\u2192\n   487\u2192        if weight_decay != 0 or maximize:\n   488\u2192            # Again, reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated\n   489\u2192            torch._foreach_mul_(device_grads, minus_clr)\n   490\u2192            numerator = device_grads\n   491\u2192        else:\n   492\u2192            numerator = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, minus_clr)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   493\u2192\n   494\u2192        torch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, numerator, std)\n   495\u2192\n   496\u2192\n   497\u2192def _fused_adagrad(\n   498\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   499\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   500\u2192    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n   501\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   502\u2192    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n   503\u2192    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n   504\u2192    *,\n   505\u2192    lr: float,\n   506\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   507\u2192    lr_decay: float,\n   508\u2192    eps: float,\n   509\u2192    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n   510\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   511\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   512\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   513\u2192) -> None:\n   514\u2192    if not params:\n   515\u2192        return\n   516\u2192    if has_sparse_grad or has_complex:\n   517\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support sparse grad or complex param\")\n   518\u2192\n   519\u2192    if differentiable:\n   520\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\n   521\u2192            \"adagrad with fused=True does not support differentiable=True\"\n   522\u2192        )\n   523\u2192\n   524\u2192    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   525\u2192\n   526\u2192    grad_scale_dict = (\n   527\u2192        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else None\n   528\u2192    )\n   529\u2192    found_inf_dict = {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else None\n   530\u2192\n   531\u2192    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   532\u2192        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   533\u2192    )\n   534\u2192    for (device, _), (\n   535\u2192        (\n   536\u2192            device_params_,\n   537\u2192            device_grads_,\n   538\u2192            device_state_sums_,\n   539\u2192            device_state_steps_,\n   540\u2192        ),\n   541\u2192        _,\n   542\u2192    ) in grouped_tensors.items():\n   543\u2192        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n   544\u2192        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n   545\u2192        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n   546\u2192        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n   547\u2192\n   548\u2192        device_grad_scale, device_found_inf = None, None\n   549\u2192        if grad_scale is not None and grad_scale_dict is not None:\n   550\u2192            if device not in grad_scale_dict:\n   551\u2192                grad_scale_dict[device] = grad_scale.to(device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[index]\n   552\u2192            device_grad_scale = grad_scale_dict[device]  # type: ignore[index]\n   553\u2192        if found_inf is not None and found_inf_dict is not None:\n   554\u2192            if found_inf not in found_inf_dict:\n   555\u2192                found_inf_dict[device] = found_inf.to(device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[index]\n   556\u2192            device_found_inf = found_inf_dict[device]  # type: ignore[index]\n   557\u2192        torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n   558\u2192        torch._fused_adagrad_(\n   559\u2192            device_params,\n   560\u2192            device_grads,\n   561\u2192            device_state_sums,\n   562\u2192            device_state_steps,\n   563\u2192            lr=lr,\n   564\u2192            lr_decay=lr_decay,\n   565\u2192            weight_decay=weight_decay,\n   566\u2192            eps=eps,\n   567\u2192            maximize=maximize,\n   568\u2192            grad_scale=device_grad_scale,\n   569\u2192            found_inf=device_found_inf,\n   570\u2192        )\n   571\u2192        if device_found_inf is not None:\n   572\u2192            torch._foreach_sub_(\n   573\u2192                device_state_steps, [device_found_inf] * len(device_state_steps)\n   574\u2192            )\n   575\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 29\u2192\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192__all__ = [\"Adam\", \"adam\"]\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192class Adam(Optimizer):\n    35\u2192    def __init__(\n    36\u2192        self,\n    37\u2192        params: ParamsT,\n    38\u2192        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-3,\n    39\u2192        betas: tuple[Union[float, Tensor], Union[float, Tensor]] = (0.9, 0.999),\n    40\u2192        eps: float = 1e-8,\n    41\u2192        weight_decay: float = 0,\n    42\u2192        amsgrad: bool = False,\n    43\u2192        *,\n    44\u2192        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    45\u2192        maximize: bool = False,\n    46\u2192        capturable: bool = False,\n    47\u2192        differentiable: bool = False,\n    48\u2192        fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    49\u2192        decoupled_weight_decay: bool = False,\n    50\u2192    ):\n    51\u2192        if isinstance(lr, Tensor):\n    52\u2192            if foreach and not capturable:\n    53\u2192                raise ValueError(\n    54\u2192                    \"lr as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n    55\u2192                )\n    56\u2192            if lr.numel() != 1:\n    57\u2192                raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n    58\u2192        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n    59\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n    60\u2192        if not 0.0 <= eps:\n    61\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid epsilon value: {eps}\")\n    62\u2192        if not 0.0 <= betas[0] < 1.0:\n    63\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid beta parameter at index 0: {betas[0]}\")\n    64\u2192        if not 0.0 <= betas[1] < 1.0:\n    65\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid beta parameter at index 1: {betas[1]}\")\n    66\u2192        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n    67\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n    68\u2192        if not (\n    69\u2192           raise ValueError(\"betas must be either both floats or both Tensors\")\n    73\u2192        if isinstance(betas[0], Tensor):\n    74\u2192            if not capturable and foreach:\n    75\u2192                raise ValueError(\n    76\u2192                    \"betas[0] as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n    77\u2192                )\n    78\u2192 if not capturable and foreach:\n    82\u2192                raise ValueError(\n    83\u2192                    \"betas[1] as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n    84\u2192                )\n    85\u2192            if betas[1].numel() != 1:\n    86\u2192                raise ValueError(\"Tensor betas[1] must be 1-element\")\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192 does not support `differentiable`\")\n   106\u2192            self._step_supports_amp_scaling = True\n   107\u2192            # TODO(crcrpar): [low prec params & their higher prec copy]\n   108\u2192 loss of information.\n   111\u2192            if foreach:\n   112\u2192                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` and `foreach` cannot be `True` together.\")\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192    def __setstate__(self, state):\n   115\u2192        super().__setstate__(state)\n   116\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n   117\u2192            group.setdefault(\"amsgrad\", False)\n   118\u2192            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n   119\u2192            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n   120\u2192            group.setdefault(\"capturable\", False)\n   121\u2192 124\u2192            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n   125\u2192                p_state = self.state.get(p, [])\n   126\u2192                if len(p_state) != 0 and not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n   127\u2192                    step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n   128\u2192                    p_state[\"step\"] = (\n   129\u2192                        torch.tensor(\n   130\u2192                            step_val,\n   131\u2192                            dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(is_fused=fused),\n   132\u2192                            device=p.device,\n   133\u2192                        )\n   134\u2192                        if group[\"capturable\"] or group[\"fused\"]\n   135\u2192                        else torch.tensor(step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   136\u2192                    )\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    def _init_group(\n   139\u2192        self,\n   140\u2192        group,\n 155\u2192                        \"Adam does not support sparse gradients, please consider SparseAdam instead\"\n   156\u2192                    )\n   157\u2192                grads.append(p.grad)\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192                state = self.state[p]\n   160\u2192                # Lazy state initialization\n   161\u2192                if len(state) == 0:\n   162\u2192 group[\"capturable\"] or group[\"fused\"]\n   174\u2192                        else torch.tensor(0.0, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   175\u2192                    )\n   176\u2192                    # Exponential moving average of gradient values\n   177\u2192                    state[\"exp_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   178\u2192                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   179\u2192                    )\n   180\u2192                    # Exponential moving average of squared gradient values\n   181\u2192                    state[\"exp_avg_sq\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   182\u2192                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   183\u2192                    )\n   184\u2192                    if group[\"amsgrad\"]:\n   185\u2192                        # Maintains max of all exp. moving avg. of sq. grad. values\n   186\u2192                        state[\"max_exp_avg_sq\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   187\u2192                            p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   188\u2192                        )\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192                exp_avgs.append(state[\"exp_avg\"])\n   191\u2192                exp_avg_sqs.append(state[\"exp_avg_sq\"])\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192                if group[\"amsgrad\"]:\n   194\u2192                    max_exp_avg_sqs.append(state[\"max_exp_avg_sq\"])\n   195\u2192                if group[\"differentiable\"] and state[\"step\"].requires_grad:\n   196\u2192                    raise RuntimeError(\n   197\u2192                        \"`requires_grad` is not supported for `step` in differentiable mode\"\n   198\u2192                    )\n   199\u2192\n   200\u2192                # Foreach without capturable does not support a tensor lr\n   201\u2192                if (\n   202\u2192                    group[\"foreach\"]\n   203\u2192                    and torch.is_tensor(group[\"lr\"])\n   204\u2192                    and not group[\"capturable\"]\n   205\u2192                ):\n   206\u2192                    raise RuntimeError(\n   207\u2192                        \"lr as a Tensor is not supported for capturable=False and foreach=True\"\n   208\u2192                    )\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192                state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n   211\u2192        return has_complex\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n   214\u2192    def step(self, closure=None):\n   215\u2192        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192        Args:\n   218\u2192            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n   219\u2192                and returns the loss.\n   220\u2192        \"\"\"\n   221\u2192        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n   222\u2192\n   223\u2192        loss = None\n   224\u2192        if closure is not None:\n   225\u2192            with torch.enable_grad():\n   226\u2192                loss = closure()\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n   229\u2192            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n   230\u2192            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n   231\u2192            exp_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n   232\u2192            exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor] = []\n   233\u2192            max_exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor] = []\n   234\u2192
