7\u2192from .optimizer import (\n     8\u2192    _capturable_doc,\n     9\u2192    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    10\u2192    _differentiable_doc,\n    11\u2192    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    12\u2192    _foreach_doc,\n    13\u2192    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    14\u2192    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    15\u2192    _get_value,\n    16\u2192    _maximize_doc,\n    17\u2192    _params_doc,\n    18\u2192    _to_scalar,\n    19\u2192    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    20\u2192    _view_as_real,\n    21\u2192    Optimizer,\n    22\u2192    ParamsT,\n    23\u2192)\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192__all__ = [\"ASGD\", \"asgd\"]\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192class ASGD(Optimizer):\n    30\u2192    def __init__(\n    31\u2192        self,\n    32\u2192        params: ParamsT,\n    33\u2192        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-2,\n    34\u2192        lambd: float = 1e-4,\n    35\u2192        alpha: float = 0.75,\n    36\u2192        t0: float = 1e6,\n    37\u2192        weight_decay: float = 0,\n    38\u2192        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    39\u2192        maximize: bool = False,\n    40\u2192        differentiable: bool = False,\n    41\u2192        capturable: bool = False,\n    42\u2192    ):\n    43\u2192        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n    44\u2192            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n    45\u2192        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n    46\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n    47\u2192        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n    48\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192        defaults = {\n    51\u2192            \"lr\": lr,\n    52\u2192            \"lambd\": lambd,\n    53\u2192            \"alpha\": alpha,\n    54\u2192            \"t0\": t0,\n    55\u2192            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n    56\u2192            \"foreach\": foreach,\n    57\u2192            \"maximize\": maximize,\n    58\u2192            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n    59\u2192            \"capturable\": capturable,\n    60\u2192        }\n    61\u2192        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192    def __setstate__(self, state):\n    64\u2192        super().__setstate__(state)\n    65\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n    66\u2192            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n    67\u2192            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n    68\u2192            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n    69\u2192            group.setdefault(\"capturable\", False)\n    70\u2192            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n    71\u2192                p_state = self.state.get(p, [])\n    72\u2192                if len(p_state) != 0:\n    73\u2192                    if not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n    74\u2192                        step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n    75\u2192                        p_state[\"step\"] = torch.tensor(\n    76\u2192                            step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n    77\u2192                        )\n    78\u2192                    if not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"eta\"]):\n    79\u2192                        p_state[\"eta\"] = torch.tensor(\n    80\u2192                            p_state[\"eta\"], dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n    81\u2192                        )\n    82\u2192                    if not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"mu\"]):\n    83\u2192                        p_state[\"mu\"] = torch.tensor(\n    84\u2192                            p_state[\"mu\"], dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n    85\u2192                        )\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192    def _init_group(self, group, params_with_grad, grads, mus, axs, etas, state_steps):\n    88\u2192        has_complex = False\n    89\u2192        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n    90\u2192            if p.grad is not None:\n    91\u2192                has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n    92\u2192                params_with_grad.append(p)\n    93\u2192                if p.grad.is_sparse:\n    94\u2192                    raise RuntimeError(\"ASGD does not support sparse gradients\")\n    95\u2192                grads.append(p.grad)\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192                state = self.state[p]\n    98\u2192                # State initialization\n    99\u2192                if len(state) == 0:\n   100\u2192                    state[\"step\"] = torch.zeros(\n   101\u2192                        (), device=p.device, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype()\n   102\u2192                    )\n   103\u2192                    state[\"eta\"] = (\n   104\u2192                        torch.as_tensor(\n   105\u2192                            _to_scalar(group[\"lr\"]),\n   106\u2192                            device=p.device,\n   107\u2192                            dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(),\n   108\u2192                        )\n   109\u2192                        .clone()\n   110\u2192                        .detach()\n   111\u2192                    )\n   112\u2192                    state[\"mu\"] = torch.ones(\n   113\u2192                        (), device=p.device, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype()\n   114\u2192                    )\n   115\u2192                    state[\"ax\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   116\u2192                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   117\u2192                    )\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192                mus.append(state[\"mu\"])\n   120\u2192                axs.append(state[\"ax\"])\n   121\u2192                etas.append(state[\"eta\"])\n   122\u2192                state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n   123\u2192        return has_complex\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n   126\u2192    def step(self, closure=None):\n   127\u2192        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192        Args:\n   130\u2192            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n   131\u2192                and returns the loss.\n   132\u2192        \"\"\"\n   133\u2192        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192        loss = None\n   136\u2192        if closure is not None:\n   137\u2192            with torch.enable_grad():\n   138\u2192                loss = closure()\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n   141\u2192            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n   142\u2192            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n   143\u2192            mus: list[Tensor] = []\n   144\u2192            axs: list[Tensor] = []\n   145\u2192            etas: list[Tensor] = []\n   146\u2192            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192            has_complex = self._init_group(\n   149\u2192                group, params_with_grad, grads, mus, axs, etas, state_steps\n   150\u2192            )\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192            asgd(\n   153\u2192                params_with_grad,\n   154\u2192                grads,\n   155\u2192                axs,\n   156\u2192                mus,\n   157\u2192                etas,\n   158\u2192                state_steps,\n   159\u2192                lambd=group[\"lambd\"],\n   160\u2192                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n   161\u2192                t0=group[\"t0\"],\n   162\u2192                alpha=group[\"alpha\"],\n   163\u2192                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n   164\u2192                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n   165\u2192                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n   166\u2192                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n   167\u2192                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n   168\u2192                has_complex=has_complex,\n   169\u2192            )\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192        return loss\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192ASGD.__doc__ = rf\"\"\"Implements Averaged Stochastic Gradient Descent.\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192    It has been proposed in `Acceleration of stochastic approximation by\n   177\u2192    averaging`_.\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192    Args:\n   180\u2192        {_params_doc}\n   181\u2192        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n   182\u2192        lambd (float, optional): decay term (default: 1e-4)\n   183\u2192        alpha (float, optional): power for eta update (default: 0.75)\n   184\u2192        t0 (float, optional): point at which to start averaging (default: 1e6)\n   185\u2192        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   186\u2192        {_foreach_doc}\n   187\u2192        {_maximize_doc}\n   188\u2192        {_differentiable_doc}\n   189\u2192        {_capturable_doc}\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192    .. _Acceleration of stochastic approximation by averaging:\n   192\u2192        https://meyn.ece.ufl.edu/wp-content/uploads/sites/77/archive/spm_files/Courses/ECE555-2011/555media/poljud92.pdf\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192    \"\"\"\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192def _single_tensor_asgd(\n   198\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   199\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   200\u2192    axs: list[Tensor],\n   201\u2192    mus: list[Tensor],\n   202\u2192    etas: list[Tensor],\n   203\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   204\u2192    *,\n   205\u2192    lambd: float,\n   206\u2192    lr: float,\n   207\u2192    t0: float,\n   208\u2192    alpha: float,\n   209\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   210\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   211\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   212\u2192    capturable: bool,\n   213\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   214\u2192):\n   215\u2192    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   216\u2192        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   219\u2192        grad = grads[i]\n   220\u2192        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n   221\u2192        mu = mus[i]\n   222\u2192        ax = axs[i]\n   223\u2192        eta = etas[i]\n   224\u2192        step_t = state_steps[i]\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   227\u2192        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   228\u2192            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   229\u2192            assert (\n   230\u2192                param.device.type\n   231\u2192                == mu.device.type\n   232\u2192                == eta.device.type\n   233\u2192                == step_t.device.type\n   234\u2192                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   235\u2192            ), (\n   236\u2192                f\"If capturable=True, params, mus, etas, and state_steps must be \"\n   237\u2192                f\"on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   238\u2192            )\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192        if torch.is_complex(param):\n   241\u2192            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   242\u2192            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   243\u2192            ax = torch.view_as_real(ax)\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192        # update step\n   246\u2192        step_t += 1\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192        if weight_decay != 0:\n   249\u2192            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192        if capturable:\n   252\u2192            param.mul_(1 - lambd * eta)\n   253\u2192            param.addcmul_(grad, eta, value=-1)  # update parameter\n   254\u2192        else:\n   255\u2192            eta_value = _get_value(eta)\n   256\u2192            param.mul_(1 - lambd * eta_value)  # decay term\n   257\u2192            param.add_(grad, alpha=-eta_value)  # update parameter\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192        # averaging\n   260\u2192        if capturable or mu.item() != 1:\n   261\u2192            ax.add_(param.sub(ax).mul_(mu))\n   262\u2192        else:\n   263\u2192            ax.copy_(param)\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192        if capturable:\n   266\u2192            eta.copy_(lr / ((1 + lambd * lr * step_t) ** alpha))\n   267\u2192            mu.copy_(1 / torch.maximum(step_t - t0, torch.ones_like(step_t)))\n   268\u2192        else:\n   269\u2192            step = _get_value(step_t)\n   270\u2192            new_eta = torch.as_tensor(lr / ((1 + lambd * lr * step) ** alpha))\n   271\u2192            eta.copy_(new_eta)\n   272\u2192            new_mu = torch.as_tensor(1 / max(1, step - t0))\n   273\u2192            mu.copy_(new_mu)\n   274\u2192\n   275\u2192\n   276\u2192def _multi_tensor_asgd(\n   277\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   278\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   279\u2192    axs: list[Tensor],\n   280\u2192    mus: list[Tensor],\n   281\u2192    etas: list[Tensor],\n   282\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   283\u2192    *,\n   284\u2192    lambd: float,\n   285\u2192    lr: float,\n   286\u2192    t0: float,\n   287\u2192    alpha: float,\n   288\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   289\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   290\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   291\u2192    capturable: bool,\n   292\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   293\u2192):\n   294\u2192    if len(params) == 0:\n   295\u2192        return\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   298\u2192\n   299\u2192    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   300\u2192    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   301\u2192        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n   302\u2192            supports_xla=False\n   303\u2192        )\n   304\u2192        assert all(\n   305\u2192            p.device.type == mu.device.type == eta.device.type == step.device.type\n   306\u2192            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   307\u2192            for p, mu, eta, step in zip(params, mus, etas, state_steps)\n   308\u2192        ), (\n   309\u2192            f\"If capturable=True, params, mus, etas, and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   310\u2192        )\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   315\u2192        [params, grads, axs, mus, etas, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   316\u2192    )\n   317\u2192    for (device, _), (\n   318\u2192        (\n   319\u2192            grouped_params_,\n   320\u2192            grouped_grads_,\n   321\u2192            grouped_axs_,\n   322\u2192            grouped_mus_,\n   323\u2192            grouped_etas_,\n   324\u2192            grouped_state_steps_,\n   325\u2192        ),\n   326\u2192        _,\n   327\u2192    ) in grouped_tensors.items():\n   328\u2192        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n   329\u2192        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n   330\u2192        grouped_axs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_axs_)\n   331\u2192        grouped_mus = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_mus_)\n   332\u2192        grouped_etas = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_etas_)\n   333\u2192        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192        if has_complex:\n   336\u2192            _view_as_real(grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_axs)\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192        if maximize:\n   339\u2192            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   340\u2192\n   341\u2192        # Update steps\n   342\u2192        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   343\u2192        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   344\u2192        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   345\u2192        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   346\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(\n   347\u2192                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   348\u2192            )\n   349\u2192        else:\n   350\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   351\u2192\n   352\u2192        # intermediate = grad + param * lambd\n   353\u2192        intermediate: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   354\u2192        if weight_decay != 0:\n   355\u2192            if maximize:\n   356\u2192                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n   357\u2192                intermediate = grouped_grads\n   358\u2192            else:\n   359\u2192                intermediate = torch._foreach_add(\n   360\u2192                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   361\u2192                )\n   362\u2192\n   363\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(intermediate, grouped_params, alpha=lambd)\n   364\u2192        else:\n   365\u2192            intermediate = torch._foreach_add(\n   366\u2192                grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=lambd\n   367\u2192            )\n   368\u2192\n   369\u2192        # update param\n   370\u2192        # param * (1 - lambd * eta) - eta * grad\n   371\u2192        # => param - param * lambd * eta - eta * grad\n   372\u2192        # => param - eta * intermediate\n   373\u2192        torch._foreach_addcmul_(grouped_params, intermediate, grouped_etas, value=-1)\n   374\u2192        del intermediate\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192        # update grouped_axs\n   377\u2192        # averaging: ax = ax + mu * (param - ax)\n   378\u2192        # Note (mlazos): We can't use lerp here since it requires weight to be float64\n   379\u2192        # and our grouping code requires dtypes to match for all tensors in a group (and it should, since\n   380\u2192        # we use the mus in other places)\n   381\u2192        # all dtypes need to match, so we could introduce a cast in a loop\n   382\u2192        # but since this only adds one additional kernel launch, this looks like the cleaner\n   383\u2192        # and faster solution\n   384\u2192        intermediate = torch._foreach_sub(grouped_params, grouped_axs)\n   385\u2192        torch._foreach_addcmul_(grouped_axs, intermediate, grouped_mus)\n   386\u2192        del intermediate\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192        new_etas: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   389\u2192        new_mus: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   390\u2192        if capturable:\n   391\u2192            # update grouped_mus\n   392\u2192            new_mus = torch._foreach_sub(grouped_state_steps, t0)\n   393\u2192            torch._foreach_maximum_(new_mus, 1.0)\n   394\u2192            torch._foreach_reciprocal_(new_mus)\n   395\u2192            torch._foreach_copy_(grouped_mus, new_mus)\n   396\u2192            del new_mus\n   397\u2192\n   398\u2192            # update eta = lr / ((1 + lambd * lr * step)^alpha)\n   399\u2192            new_etas = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_state_steps, lambd)\n   400\u2192            torch._foreach_mul_(new_etas, lr)\n   401\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(new_etas, 1)\n   402\u2192            torch._foreach_pow_(new_etas, alpha)\n   403\u2192            torch._foreach_reciprocal_(new_etas)\n   404\u2192            torch._foreach_mul_(new_etas, lr)\n   405\u2192            torch._foreach_copy_(grouped_etas, new_etas)\n   406\u2192        else:\n   407\u2192            new_etas = [\n   408\u2192                torch.as_tensor(lr / ((1 + lambd * lr * step) ** alpha), device=device)\n   409\u2192                for step in grouped_state_steps\n   410\u2192            ]\n   411\u2192            new_mus = [\n   412\u2192                torch.as_tensor(1 / max(1, _get_value(step) - t0), device=device)\n   413\u2192                for step in grouped_state_steps\n   414\u2192            ]\n   415\u2192            torch._foreach_copy_(grouped_etas, new_etas)\n   416\u2192            torch._foreach_copy_(grouped_mus, new_mus)\n   417\u2192\n   418\u2192\n   419\u2192@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_asgd)\n   420\u2192def asgd(\n   421\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   422\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   423\u2192    axs: list[Tensor],\n   424\u2192    mus: list[Tensor],\n   425\u2192    etas: list[Tensor],\n   426\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   427\u2192    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n   428\u2192    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n   429\u2192    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n   430\u2192    maximize: bool = False,\n   431\u2192    differentiable: bool = False,\n   432\u2192    capturable: bool = False,\n   433\u2192    has_complex: bool = False,\n   434\u2192    *,\n   435\u2192    lambd: float,\n   436\u2192    lr: float,\n   437\u2192    t0: float,\n   438\u2192    alpha: float,\n   439\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   440\u2192):\n   441\u2192    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs asgd algorithm computation.\n   442\u2192\n   443\u2192    See :class:`~torch.optim.ASGD` for details.\n   444\u2192    \"\"\"\n   445\u2192    if foreach is None:\n   446\u2192        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n   447\u2192            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n   448\u2192        )\n   449\u2192\n   450\u2192    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   451\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n   452\u2192\n   453\u2192    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   454\u2192        func = _multi_tensor_asgd\n   455\u2192    else:\n   456\u2192        func = _single_tensor_asgd\n   457\u2192\n   458\u2192    func(\n   459\u2192        params,\n   460\u2192        grads,\n   461\u2192        axs,\n   462\u2192        mus,\n   463\u2192        etas,\n   464\u2192        state_steps,\n   465\u2192        lambd=lambd,\n   466\u2192        lr=lr,\n   467\u2192        t0=t0,\n   468\u2192        alpha=alpha,\n   469\u2192        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n   470\u2192        maximize=maximize,\n   471\u2192        differentiable=differentiable,\n   472\u2192        capturable=capturable,\n   473\u2192        has_complex=has_complex,\n   474\u2192    )\n   475\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
19. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/optim/nadam.py"
}
Output
"     1\u2192# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\n     2\u2192r\"\"\"Implementation for the NAdam algorithm.\"\"\"\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192from typing import cast, Optional, Union\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import torch\n     7\u2192from torch import Tensor\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192from .optimizer import (\n    10\u2192    _capturable_doc,\n    11\u2192    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    12\u2192    _differentiable_doc,\n    13\u2192    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    14\u2192    _foreach_doc,\n    15\u2192    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    16\u2192    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    17\u2192    _get_value,\n    18\u2192    _maximize_doc,\n    19\u2192    _params_doc,\n    20\u2192    _stack_if_compiling,\n    21\u2192    _to_scalar,\n    22\u2192    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    23\u2192    _view_as_real,\n    24\u2192    Optimizer,\n    25\u2192    ParamsT,\n    26\u2192)\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192__all__ = [\"NAdam\", \"nadam\"]\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192class NAdam(Optimizer):  # noqa: D101\n    33\u2192    def __init__(\n    34\u2192        self,\n    35\u2192        params: ParamsT,\n    36\u2192        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 2e-3,\n    37\u2192        betas: tuple[float, float] = (0.9, 0.999),\n    38\u2192        eps: float = 1e-8,\n    39\u2192        weight_decay: float = 0,\n    40\u2192        momentum_decay: float = 4e-3,\n    41\u2192        decoupled_weight_decay: bool = False,\n    42\u2192        *,\n    43\u2192        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    44\u2192        maximize: bool = False,\n    45\u2192        capturable: bool = False,\n    46\u2192        differentiable: bool = False,\n    47\u2192    ):  # noqa: D107\n    48\u2192        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n    49\u2192            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n    50\u2192        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n    51\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n    52\u2192        if not 0.0 <= eps:\n    53\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid epsilon value: {eps}\")\n    54\u2192        if not 0.0 <= betas[0] < 1.0:\n    55\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid beta parameter at index 0: {betas[0]}\")\n    56\u2192        if not 0.0 <= betas[1] < 1.0:\n    57\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid beta parameter at index 1: {betas[1]}\")\n    58\u2192        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n    59\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n    60\u2192        if not 0.0 <= momentum_decay:\n    61\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid momentum_decay value: {momentum_decay}\")\n    62\u2192        defaults = {\n    63\u2192            \"lr\": lr,\n    64\u2192            \"betas\": betas,\n    65\u2192            \"eps\": eps,\n    66\u2192            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n    67\u2192            \"momentum_decay\": momentum_decay,\n    68\u2192            \"decoupled_weight_decay\": decoupled_weight_decay,\n    69\u2192            \"maximize\": maximize,\n    70\u2192            \"foreach\": foreach,\n    71\u2192            \"capturable\": capturable,\n    72\u2192            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n    73\u2192        }\n    74\u2192        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192    def __setstate__(self, state):  # noqa: D105\n    77\u2192        super().__setstate__(state)\n    78\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n    79\u2192            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n    80\u2192            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n    81\u2192            group.setdefault(\"capturable\", False)\n    82\u2192            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n    83\u2192            group.setdefault(\"decoupled_weight_decay\", False)\n    84\u2192            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n    85\u2192                p_state = self.state.get(p, [])\n    86\u2192                if len(p_state) != 0:\n    87\u2192                    if not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n    88\u2192                        step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n    89\u2192                        p_state[\"step\"] = (\n    90\u2192                            torch.tensor(\n    91\u2192                                step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n    92\u2192                            )\n    93\u2192                            if group[\"capturable\"]\n    94\u2192                            else torch.tensor(step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n    95\u2192                        )\n    96\u2192                    if not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"mu_product\"]):\n    97\u2192                        mu_prod_val = p_state[\"mu_product\"]\n    98\u2192                        p_state[\"mu_product\"] = (\n    99\u2192                            torch.tensor(\n   100\u2192                                mu_prod_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n   101\u2192                            )\n   102\u2192                            if group[\"capturable\"]\n   103\u2192                            else torch.tensor(mu_prod_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   104\u2192                        )\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192    def _init_group(\n   107\u2192        self,\n   108\u2192        group,\n   109\u2192        params_with_grad,\n   110\u2192        grads,\n   111\u2192        exp_avgs,\n   112\u2192        exp_avg_sqs,\n   113\u2192        mu_products,\n   114\u2192        state_steps,\n   115\u2192    ):\n   116\u2192        has_complex = False\n   117\u2192        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n   118\u2192            if p.grad is not None:\n   119\u2192                has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n   120\u2192                params_with_grad.append(p)\n   121\u2192                if p.grad.is_sparse:\n   122\u2192                    raise RuntimeError(\"NAdam does not support sparse gradients\")\n   123\u2192                grads.append(p.grad)\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192                state = self.state[p]\n   126\u2192                # Lazy state initialization\n   127\u2192                if len(state) == 0:\n   128\u2192                    # note(crcrpar): [special device hosting for step]\n   129\u2192                    # Deliberately host `step` and `mu_product` on CPU if capturable is False.\n   130\u2192                    # This is because kernel launches are costly on CUDA and XLA.\n   131\u2192                    state[\"step\"] = (\n   132\u2192                        torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n   133\u2192                        if group[\"capturable\"]\n   134\u2192                        else torch.tensor(0.0, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   135\u2192                    )\n   136\u2192                    state[\"mu_product\"] = (\n   137\u2192                        torch.ones((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n   138\u2192                        if group[\"capturable\"]\n   139\u2192                        else torch.tensor(1.0, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   140\u2192                    )\n   141\u2192                    # Exponential moving average of gradient values\n   142\u2192                    state[\"exp_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   143\u2192                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   144\u2192                    )\n   145\u2192                    # Exponential moving average of squared gradient values\n   146\u2192                    state[\"exp_avg_sq\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   147\u2192                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   148\u2192                    )\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192                exp_avgs.append(state[\"exp_avg\"])\n   151\u2192                exp_avg_sqs.append(state[\"exp_avg_sq\"])\n   152\u2192                mu_products.append(state[\"mu_product\"])\n   153\u2192                state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n   154\u2192        return has_complex\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n   157\u2192    def step(self, closure=None):\n   158\u2192        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192        Args:\n   161\u2192            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n   162\u2192                and returns the loss.\n   163\u2192        \"\"\"\n   164\u2192        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192        loss = None\n   167\u2192        if closure is not None:\n   168\u2192            with torch.enable_grad():\n   169\u2192                loss = closure()\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n   172\u2192            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n   173\u2192            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n   174\u2192            exp_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n   175\u2192            exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor] = []\n   176\u2192            mu_products: list[Tensor] = []\n   177\u2192            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n   178\u2192            beta1, beta2 = cast(tuple[float, float], group[\"betas\"])\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192            has_complex = self._init_group(\n   181\u2192                group,\n   182\u2192                params_with_grad,\n   183\u2192                grads,\n   184\u2192                exp_avgs,\n   185\u2192                exp_avg_sqs,\n   186\u2192                mu_products,\n   187\u2192                state_steps,\n   188\u2192            )\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192            nadam(\n   191\u2192                params_with_grad,\n   192\u2192                grads,\n   193\u2192                exp_avgs,\n   194\u2192                exp_avg_sqs,\n   195\u2192                mu_products,\n   196\u2192                state_steps,\n   197\u2192                beta1=beta1,\n   198\u2192                beta2=beta2,\n   199\u2192                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n   200\u2192                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n   201\u2192                momentum_decay=group[\"momentum_decay\"],\n   202\u2192                eps=group[\"eps\"],\n   203\u2192                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n   204\u2192                decoupled_weight_decay=group[\"decoupled_weight_decay\"],\n   205\u2192                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n   206\u2192                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n   207\u2192                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n   208\u2192                has_complex=has_complex,\n   209\u2192            )\n   210\u2192\n   211\u2192        return loss\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192NAdam.__doc__ = (\n   215\u2192    r\"\"\"Implements NAdam algorithm.\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192    .. math::\n   218\u2192       \\begin{aligned}\n   219\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   220\u2192            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma_t \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\beta_1,\\beta_2 \\text{ (betas)},\n   221\u2192                \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}                   \\\\\n   222\u2192            &\\hspace{13mm} \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)}, \\:\\psi \\text{ (momentum decay)}    \\\\\n   223\u2192            &\\hspace{13mm} \\: \\textit{decoupled\\_weight\\_decay}, \\:\\textit{maximize}             \\\\\n   224\u2192            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n   225\u2192                v_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( second moment)}                                 \\\\[-1.ex]\n   226\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   227\u2192            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   228\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n   229\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n   230\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n   231\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n   232\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1}                                       \\\\\n   233\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm} \\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                          \\\\\n   234\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{decoupled\\_weight\\_decay}                       \\\\\n   235\u2192            &\\hspace{15mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\lambda \\theta_{t-1}                    \\\\\n   236\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\n   237\u2192            &\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda \\theta_{t-1}                             \\\\\n   238\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm} \\mu_t \\leftarrow \\beta_1 \\big(1 - \\frac{1}{2}  0.96^{t \\psi} \\big)     \\\\\n   239\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm} \\mu_{t+1} \\leftarrow \\beta_1 \\big(1 - \\frac{1}{2} 0.96^{(t+1)\\psi}\\big)\\\\\n   240\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}m_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t          \\\\\n   241\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_2 v_{t-1} + (1-\\beta_2) g^2_t          \\\\\n   242\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}\\widehat{m_t} \\leftarrow \\mu_{t+1} m_t/(1-\\prod_{i=1}^{t+1}\\mu_i)\\\\[-1.ex]\n   243\u2192            & \\hspace{11mm} + (1-\\mu_t) g_t /(1-\\prod_{i=1}^{t} \\mu_{i})                         \\\\\n   244\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow   v_t/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)                   \\\\\n   245\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_t - \\gamma \\widehat{m_t}/\n   246\u2192                \\big(\\sqrt{\\widehat{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)                                       \\\\\n   247\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   248\u2192            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   249\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   250\u2192       \\end{aligned}\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Incorporating Nesterov Momentum into Adam`_.\n   253\u2192    \"\"\"\n   254\u2192    + rf\"\"\"\n   255\u2192    Args:\n   256\u2192        {_params_doc}\n   257\u2192        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 2e-3)\n   258\u2192        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n   259\u2192            running averages of gradient and its square (default: (0.9, 0.999))\n   260\u2192        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   261\u2192            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n   262\u2192        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   263\u2192        momentum_decay (float, optional): momentum momentum_decay (default: 4e-3)\n   264\u2192        decoupled_weight_decay (bool, optional): whether to decouple the weight\n   265\u2192            decay as in AdamW to obtain NAdamW. If True, the algorithm does not\n   266\u2192            accumulate weight decay in the momentum nor variance. (default: False)\n   267\u2192        {_foreach_doc}\n   268\u2192        {_maximize_doc}\n   269\u2192        {_capturable_doc}\n   270\u2192        {_differentiable_doc}\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192    .. _Incorporating Nesterov Momentum into Adam:\n   273\u2192        https://openreview.net/forum?id=OM0jvwB8jIp57ZJjtNEZ\n   274\u2192    .. _Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization:\n   275\u2192        https://arxiv.org/abs/1711.05101\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192    \"\"\"\n   278\u2192)\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192def _single_tensor_nadam(\n   282\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   283\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   284\u2192    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   285\u2192    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   286\u2192    mu_products: list[Tensor],\n   287\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   288\u2192    *,\n   289\u2192    beta1: float,\n   290\u2192    beta2: float,\n   291\u2192    lr: float,\n   292\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   293\u2192    momentum_decay: float,\n   294\u2192    eps: float,\n   295\u2192    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   296\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   297\u2192    capturable: bool,\n   298\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   299\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   300\u2192):\n   301\u2192    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   302\u2192        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   305\u2192        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n   306\u2192        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n   307\u2192        exp_avg_sq = exp_avg_sqs[i]\n   308\u2192        mu_product = mu_products[i]\n   309\u2192        step_t = state_steps[i]\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192        if torch.is_complex(param):\n   312\u2192            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   313\u2192            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   314\u2192            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n   315\u2192            exp_avg_sq = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg_sq)\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   318\u2192        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   319\u2192            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   320\u2192            assert (\n   321\u2192                param.device.type == mu_product.device.type == step_t.device.type\n   322\u2192                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   323\u2192            ), (\n   324\u2192                f\"If capturable=True, params, mu_products and state_steps must be \"\n   325\u2192                f\"on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   326\u2192            )\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192        # update step\n   329\u2192        step_t += 1\n   330\u2192\n   331\u2192        if capturable:\n   332\u2192            step = step_t\n   333\u2192        else:\n   334\u2192            step = _get_value(step_t)\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192        bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192        if weight_decay != 0:\n   339\u2192            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n   340\u2192                # Perform stepweight decay\n   341\u2192                param.mul_(1 - lr * weight_decay)\n   342\u2192            else:\n   343\u2192                grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192        # calculate the momentum cache \\mu^{t} and \\mu^{t+1}\n   346\u2192        mu = beta1 * (1.0 - 0.5 * (0.96 ** (step * momentum_decay)))\n   347\u2192        mu_next = beta1 * (1.0 - 0.5 * (0.96 ** ((step + 1) * momentum_decay)))\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192        # update mu_product\n   350\u2192        mu_product *= mu\n   351\u2192\n   352\u2192        # decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   353\u2192        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - beta1)\n   354\u2192        exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - beta2)\n   355\u2192        denom = exp_avg_sq.div(bias_correction2).sqrt()\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192        if differentiable or capturable:\n   358\u2192            denom = denom.add(eps)\n   359\u2192            # Make autograd track the operations\n   360\u2192            # by updating the grad and exp_avg directly and not using the\n   361\u2192            # scalar \"value\" argument of addcdiv.\n   362\u2192            mu_product_next = mu_product * mu_next\n   363\u2192            grad = grad * (-lr * (1.0 - mu) / (1.0 - mu_product))\n   364\u2192            exp_avg = exp_avg * (-lr * mu_next / (1.0 - mu_product_next))\n   365\u2192            param.addcdiv_(grad, denom)\n   366\u2192            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom)\n   367\u2192        else:\n   368\u2192            mu_product_next = _get_value(mu_product) * mu_next\n   369\u2192            denom.add_(eps)\n   370\u2192            param.addcdiv_(\n   371\u2192                grad, denom, value=(-lr * (1.0 - mu) / (1.0 - _get_value(mu_product)))\n   372\u2192            )\n   373\u2192            param.addcdiv_(\n   374\u2192                exp_avg,\n   375\u2192                denom,\n   376\u2192                value=cast(float, (-lr * mu_next) / (1.0 - mu_product_next)),\n   377\u2192            )\n   378\u2192\n   379\u2192\n   380\u2192def _multi_tensor_nadam(\n   381\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   382\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   383\u2192    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   384\u2192    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   385\u2192    mu_products: list[Tensor],\n   386\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   387\u2192    *,\n   388\u2192    beta1: float,\n   389\u2192    beta2: float,\n   390\u2192    lr: float,\n   391\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   392\u2192    momentum_decay: float,\n   393\u2192    eps: float,\n   394\u2192    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   395\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   396\u2192    capturable: bool,\n   397\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   398\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   399\u2192):\n   400\u2192    if len(params) == 0:\n   401\u2192        return\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   404\u2192\n   405\u2192    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   406\u2192    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   407\u2192        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n   408\u2192            supports_xla=False\n   409\u2192        )\n   410\u2192        assert all(\n   411\u2192            p.device.type == mp.device.type == step.device.type\n   412\u2192            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   413\u2192            for p, mp, step in zip(params, mu_products, state_steps)\n   414\u2192        ), (\n   415\u2192            \"If capturable=True, \"\n   416\u2192            \"params, mu_products, and state_steps must be on supported devices: \"\n   417\u2192            f\"{capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   418\u2192        )\n   419\u2192\n   420\u2192    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   421\u2192\n   422\u2192    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   423\u2192        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_avg_sqs, mu_products, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   424\u2192    )\n   425\u2192    for (\n   426\u2192        grouped_params_,\n   427\u2192        grouped_grads_,\n   428\u2192        grouped_exp_avgs_,\n   429\u2192        grouped_exp_avg_sqs_,\n   430\u2192        grouped_mu_products_,\n   431\u2192        grouped_state_steps_,\n   432\u2192    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n   433\u2192        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n   434\u2192        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n   435\u2192        grouped_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avgs_)\n   436\u2192        grouped_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   437\u2192        grouped_mu_products = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_mu_products_)\n   438\u2192        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192        # handle complex\n   441\u2192        if has_complex:\n   442\u2192            _view_as_real(\n   443\u2192                grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_avg_sqs\n   444\u2192            )\n   445\u2192\n   446\u2192        if maximize:\n   447\u2192            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   448\u2192\n   449\u2192        # Update steps\n   450\u2192        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   451\u2192        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   452\u2192        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   453\u2192        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   454\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(\n   455\u2192                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   456\u2192            )\n   457\u2192        else:\n   458\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   459\u2192\n   460\u2192        if weight_decay != 0:\n   461\u2192            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n   462\u2192                # Perform stepweight decay\n   463\u2192                torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_params, 1 - lr * weight_decay)\n   464\u2192            else:\n   465\u2192                # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n   466\u2192                if maximize:\n   467\u2192                    torch._foreach_add_(\n   468\u2192                        grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   469\u2192                    )\n   470\u2192                else:\n   471\u2192                    grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   472\u2192                        grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   473\u2192                    )\n   474\u2192\n   475\u2192        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   476\u2192        torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - beta1)\n   477\u2192\n   478\u2192        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_exp_avg_sqs, beta2)\n   479\u2192        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n   480\u2192            grouped_exp_avg_sqs, grouped_grads, grouped_grads, 1 - beta2\n   481\u2192        )\n   482\u2192\n   483\u2192        exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(grouped_exp_avg_sqs)\n   484\u2192\n   485\u2192        bias_correction_sqrt: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   486\u2192        mus: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   487\u2192        mu_nexts: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   488\u2192        if capturable:\n   489\u2192            # mus will be beta1 * (1 - 0.5 * 0.96 ** (step * momentum_decay))\n   490\u2192            exponent = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_state_steps, momentum_decay)\n   491\u2192            mus = torch._foreach_pow(0.96, exponent)\n   492\u2192            torch._foreach_mul_(mus, -0.5)\n   493\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(mus, 1.0)\n   494\u2192            torch._foreach_mul_(mus, beta1)\n   495\u2192\n   496\u2192            # mu_nexts will be beta1 * (1 - 0.5 * 0.96 ** ((step + 1) * momentum_decay))\n   497\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(exponent, momentum_decay)\n   498\u2192            mu_nexts = torch._foreach_pow(0.96, exponent)\n   499\u2192            torch._foreach_mul_(mu_nexts, -0.5)\n   500\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(mu_nexts, 1.0)\n   501\u2192            torch._foreach_mul_(mu_nexts, beta1)\n   502\u2192\n   503\u2192            # save peak memory as we don't need exponent anymore\n   504\u2192            del exponent\n   505\u2192\n   506\u2192            bias_correction_sqrt = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, grouped_state_steps)\n   507\u2192            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n   508\u2192            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction_sqrt, 1.0)\n   509\u2192            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction_sqrt)\n   510\u2192            torch._foreach_sqrt_(bias_correction_sqrt)\n   511\u2192        else:\n   512\u2192            bias_correction_sqrt = [\n   513\u2192                (1 - beta2 ** _get_value(step)) ** 0.5 for step in grouped_state_steps\n   514\u2192            ]\n   515\u2192            mus = [\n   516\u2192                beta1 * (1.0 - 0.5 * (0.96 ** (_get_value(step) * momentum_decay)))\n   517\u2192                for step in grouped_state_steps\n   518\u2192            ]\n   519\u2192            mu_nexts = [\n   520\u2192                beta1\n   521\u2192                * (1.0 - 0.5 * (0.96 ** ((_get_value(step) + 1) * momentum_decay)))\n   522\u2192                for step in grouped_state_steps\n   523\u2192            ]\n   524\u2192\n   525\u2192        # update mu_products\n   526\u2192        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_mu_products, mus)\n   527\u2192\n   528\u2192        torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, bias_correction_sqrt)\n   529\u2192        torch._foreach_add_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, eps)\n   530\u2192\n   531\u2192        # explicitly delete bias_correction refs to save memory\n   532\u2192        del bias_correction_sqrt\n   533\u2192\n   534\u2192        if capturable:\n   535\u2192            # Build up the step_size multiplier for grad, reusing mus' memory\n   536\u2192            torch._foreach_sub_(mus, 1.0)\n   537\u2192            torch._foreach_mul_(mus, lr)\n   538\u2192            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n   539\u2192            denom = torch._foreach_sub(grouped_mu_products, 1.0)\n   540\u2192            torch._foreach_neg_(denom)\n   541\u2192            torch._foreach_div_(mus, denom)\n   542\u2192            # - lr * (1 - mu) / (1 - mu_product)\n   543\u2192            step_size_grads = mus\n   544\u2192            # explicitly delete denom to save memory\n   545\u2192            del denom\n   546\u2192\n   547\u2192            # Build up the step_size multiplier for exp_avg, reusing mu_nexts' memory\n   548\u2192            denom = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_mu_products, mu_nexts)\n   549\u2192            torch._foreach_mul_(mu_nexts, lr)\n   550\u2192            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg, but it's okay because\n   551\u2192            # we need a negative here anyway\n   552\u2192            torch._foreach_sub_(denom, 1.0)\n   553\u2192            torch._foreach_div_(mu_nexts, denom)\n   554\u2192            # - lr * mu_next / (1 - mu_product * mu_next)\n   555\u2192            step_size_expavg = mu_nexts\n   556\u2192            # explicitly delete denom to save memory\n   557\u2192            del denom\n   558\u2192\n   559\u2192            # we cannot inplace into step_size_grads cuz it is a list of ScalarTensors\n   560\u2192            # and mul'ing with grouped_grads will result in a list of bigger Tensors\n   561\u2192            numerator = torch._foreach_mul(step_size_grads, grouped_grads)\n   562\u2192            torch._foreach_addcmul_(numerator, step_size_expavg, grouped_exp_avgs)\n   563\u2192\n   564\u2192            # finally, update params\n   565\u2192            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, numerator, exp_avg_sq_sqrt)\n   566\u2192        else:\n   567\u2192            step_size_grads = _stack_if_compiling(\n   568\u2192                [\n   569\u2192                    (_get_value(lr) * (1.0 - mu) / (1.0 - _get_value(mu_product))) * -1\n   570\u2192                    for mu_product, mu in zip(grouped_mu_products, mus)\n   571\u2192                ]\n   572\u2192            )\n   573\u2192            step_size_expavg = _stack_if_compiling(\n   574\u2192                [\n   575\u2192                    (\n   576\u2192                        _get_value(lr)\n   577\u2192                        * mu_next\n   578\u2192                        / (1.0 - _get_value(mu_product) * mu_next)\n   579\u2192                    )\n   580\u2192                    * -1\n   581\u2192                    for mu_product, mu_next in zip(grouped_mu_products, mu_nexts)\n   582\u2192                ]\n   583\u2192            )\n   584\u2192\n   585\u2192            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(\n   586\u2192                grouped_params,\n   587\u2192                grouped_grads,\n   588\u2192                exp_avg_sq_sqrt,\n   589\u2192                step_size_grads,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   590\u2192            )\n   591\u2192            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(\n   592\u2192                grouped_params,\n   593\u2192                grouped_exp_avgs,\n   594\u2192                exp_avg_sq_sqrt,\n   595\u2192                step_size_expavg,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   596\u2192            )\n   597\u2192\n   598\u2192\n   599\u2192@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_nadam)\n   600\u2192def nadam(\n   601\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   602\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   603\u2192    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   604\u2192    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   605\u2192    mu_products: list[Tensor],\n   606\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   607\u2192    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n   608\u2192    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n   609\u2192    decoupled_weight_decay: bool = False,\n   610\u2192    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n   611\u2192    capturable: bool = False,\n   612\u2192    differentiable: bool = False,\n   613\u2192    has_complex: bool = False,\n   614\u2192    maximize: bool = False,\n   615\u2192    *,\n   616\u2192    beta1: float,\n   617\u2192    beta2: float,\n   618\u2192    lr: float,\n   619\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   620\u2192    momentum_decay: float,\n   621\u2192    eps: float,\n   622\u2192):\n   623\u2192    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs NAdam algorithm computation.\n   624\u2192\n   625\u2192    See :class:`~torch.optim.NAdam` for details.\n   626\u2192    \"\"\"\n   627\u2192    if not all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps):\n   628\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\n   629\u2192            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n   630\u2192        )\n   631\u2192\n   632\u2192    if not all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in mu_products):\n   633\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\n   634\u2192            \"API has changed, `mu_products` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n   635\u2192        )\n   636\u2192\n   637\u2192    if foreach is None:\n   638\u2192        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n   639\u2192            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n   640\u2192        )\n   641\u2192\n   642\u2192    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   643\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n   644\u2192\n   645\u2192    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   646\u2192        func = _multi_tensor_nadam\n   647\u2192    else:\n   648\u2192        func = _single_tensor_nadam\n   649\u2192\n   650\u2192    func(\n   651\u2192        params,\n   652\u2192        grads,\n   653\u2192        exp_avgs,\n   654\u2192        exp_avg_sqs,\n   655\u2192        mu_products,\n   656\u2192        state_steps,\n   657\u2192        beta1=beta1,\n   658\u2192        beta2=beta2,\n   659\u2192        lr=lr,\n   660\u2192        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n   661\u2192        momentum_decay=momentum_decay,\n   662\u2192        maximize=maximize,\n   663\u2192        decoupled_weight_decay=decoupled_weight_decay,\n   664\u2192        eps=eps,\n   665\u2192        capturable=capturable,\n   666\u2192        differentiable=differentiable,\n   667\u2192        has_complex=has_complex,\n   668\u2192    )\n   669\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
