21. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/optim/rmsprop.py"
}
Output
"     1\u2192# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\n     2\u2192r\"\"\"Implementation for the RMSprop algorithm.\"\"\"\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192from typing import cast, Optional, Union\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import torch\n     7\u2192from torch import Tensor\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192from .optimizer import (\n    10\u2192    _capturable_doc,\n    11\u2192    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    12\u2192    _differentiable_doc,\n    13\u2192    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    14\u2192    _foreach_doc,\n    15\u2192    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    16\u2192    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    17\u2192    _maximize_doc,\n    18\u2192    _params_doc,\n    19\u2192    _to_scalar,\n    20\u2192    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    21\u2192    _view_as_real,\n    22\u2192    Optimizer,\n    23\u2192    ParamsT,\n    24\u2192)\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192__all__ = [\"RMSprop\", \"rmsprop\"]\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192class RMSprop(Optimizer):  # noqa: D101\n    31\u2192    def __init__(\n    32\u2192        self,\n    33\u2192        params: ParamsT,\n    34\u2192        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-2,\n    35\u2192        alpha: float = 0.99,\n    36\u2192        eps: float = 1e-8,\n    37\u2192        weight_decay: float = 0,\n    38\u2192        momentum: float = 0,\n    39\u2192        centered: bool = False,\n    40\u2192        capturable: bool = False,\n    41\u2192        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    42\u2192        maximize: bool = False,\n    43\u2192        differentiable: bool = False,\n    44\u2192    ):  # noqa: D107\n    45\u2192        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n    46\u2192            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n    47\u2192        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n    48\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n    49\u2192        if not 0.0 <= eps:\n    50\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid epsilon value: {eps}\")\n    51\u2192        if not 0.0 <= momentum:\n    52\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid momentum value: {momentum}\")\n    53\u2192        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n    54\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n    55\u2192        if not 0.0 <= alpha:\n    56\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid alpha value: {alpha}\")\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192        defaults = {\n    59\u2192            \"lr\": lr,\n    60\u2192            \"momentum\": momentum,\n    61\u2192            \"alpha\": alpha,\n    62\u2192            \"eps\": eps,\n    63\u2192            \"centered\": centered,\n    64\u2192            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n    65\u2192            \"capturable\": capturable,\n    66\u2192            \"foreach\": foreach,\n    67\u2192            \"maximize\": maximize,\n    68\u2192            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n    69\u2192        }\n    70\u2192        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192    def __setstate__(self, state):  # noqa: D105\n    73\u2192        super().__setstate__(state)\n    74\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n    75\u2192            group.setdefault(\"momentum\", 0)\n    76\u2192            group.setdefault(\"centered\", False)\n    77\u2192            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n    78\u2192            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n    79\u2192            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n    80\u2192            group.setdefault(\"capturable\", False)\n    81\u2192            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n    82\u2192                p_state = self.state.get(p, [])\n    83\u2192                if len(p_state) != 0 and not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n    84\u2192                    step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n    85\u2192                    p_state[\"step\"] = (\n    86\u2192                        torch.tensor(\n    87\u2192                            step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n    88\u2192                        )\n    89\u2192                        if group[\"capturable\"]\n    90\u2192                        else torch.tensor(step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n    91\u2192                    )\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192    def _init_group(\n    94\u2192        self,\n    95\u2192        group,\n    96\u2192        params_with_grad,\n    97\u2192        grads,\n    98\u2192        square_avgs,\n    99\u2192        momentum_buffer_list,\n   100\u2192        grad_avgs,\n   101\u2192        state_steps,\n   102\u2192    ):\n   103\u2192        has_complex = False\n   104\u2192        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n   105\u2192            if p.grad is None:\n   106\u2192                continue\n   107\u2192            has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n   108\u2192            params_with_grad.append(p)\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192            if p.grad.is_sparse:\n   111\u2192                raise RuntimeError(\"RMSprop does not support sparse gradients\")\n   112\u2192            grads.append(p.grad)\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192            state = self.state[p]\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192            # State initialization\n   117\u2192            if len(state) == 0:\n   118\u2192                state[\"step\"] = (\n   119\u2192                    torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n   120\u2192                    if group[\"capturable\"]\n   121\u2192                    else torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   122\u2192                )\n   123\u2192                state[\"square_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   124\u2192                    p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   125\u2192                )\n   126\u2192                if group[\"momentum\"] > 0:\n   127\u2192                    state[\"momentum_buffer\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   128\u2192                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   129\u2192                    )\n   130\u2192                if group[\"centered\"]:\n   131\u2192                    state[\"grad_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   132\u2192                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   133\u2192                    )\n   134\u2192            square_avgs.append(state[\"square_avg\"])\n   135\u2192            state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192            if group[\"momentum\"] > 0:\n   138\u2192                momentum_buffer_list.append(state[\"momentum_buffer\"])\n   139\u2192            if group[\"centered\"]:\n   140\u2192                grad_avgs.append(state[\"grad_avg\"])\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192        return has_complex\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n   145\u2192    def step(self, closure=None):\n   146\u2192        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192        Args:\n   149\u2192            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n   150\u2192                and returns the loss.\n   151\u2192        \"\"\"\n   152\u2192        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192        loss = None\n   155\u2192        if closure is not None:\n   156\u2192            with torch.enable_grad():\n   157\u2192                loss = closure()\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n   160\u2192            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n   161\u2192            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n   162\u2192            square_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n   163\u2192            grad_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n   164\u2192            momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor] = []\n   165\u2192            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192            has_complex = self._init_group(\n   168\u2192                group,\n   169\u2192                params_with_grad,\n   170\u2192                grads,\n   171\u2192                square_avgs,\n   172\u2192                momentum_buffer_list,\n   173\u2192                grad_avgs,\n   174\u2192                state_steps,\n   175\u2192            )\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192            rmsprop(\n   178\u2192                params_with_grad,\n   179\u2192                grads,\n   180\u2192                square_avgs,\n   181\u2192                grad_avgs,\n   182\u2192                momentum_buffer_list,\n   183\u2192                state_steps,\n   184\u2192                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n   185\u2192                alpha=group[\"alpha\"],\n   186\u2192                eps=group[\"eps\"],\n   187\u2192                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n   188\u2192                momentum=group[\"momentum\"],\n   189\u2192                centered=group[\"centered\"],\n   190\u2192                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n   191\u2192                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n   192\u2192                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n   193\u2192                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n   194\u2192                has_complex=has_complex,\n   195\u2192            )\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192        return loss\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192\n   200\u2192RMSprop.__doc__ = (\n   201\u2192    r\"\"\"Implements RMSprop algorithm.\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192    .. math::\n   204\u2192       \\begin{aligned}\n   205\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   206\u2192            &\\textbf{input}      : \\alpha \\text{ (alpha)}, \\: \\gamma \\text{ (lr)},\n   207\u2192                \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}                   \\\\\n   208\u2192            &\\hspace{13mm}   \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},\\: \\mu \\text{ (momentum)},\n   209\u2192                \\: centered, \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                                       \\\\\n   210\u2192            &\\textbf{initialize} : v_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (square average)}, \\:\n   211\u2192                \\textbf{b}_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (buffer)}, \\: g^{ave}_0 \\leftarrow 0     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   212\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   213\u2192            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   214\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n   215\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                                    \\\\\n   216\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n   217\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\alpha v_{t-1} + (1 - \\alpha) g^2_t\n   218\u2192                \\hspace{8mm}                                                                     \\\\\n   219\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm} \\tilde{v_t} \\leftarrow v_t                                             \\\\\n   220\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: centered                                                          \\\\\n   221\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm} g^{ave}_t \\leftarrow g^{ave}_{t-1} \\alpha + (1-\\alpha) g_t            \\\\\n   222\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm} \\tilde{v_t} \\leftarrow \\tilde{v_t} -  \\big(g^{ave}_{t} \\big)^2        \\\\\n   223\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\mu > 0                                                           \\\\\n   224\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm} \\textbf{b}_t\\leftarrow \\mu \\textbf{b}_{t-1} +\n   225\u2192                g_t/ \\big(\\sqrt{\\tilde{v_t}} +  \\epsilon \\big)                                   \\\\\n   226\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\textbf{b}_t                \\\\\n   227\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm} else                                                                   \\\\\n   228\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm}\\theta_t      \\leftarrow   \\theta_{t-1} -\n   229\u2192                \\gamma  g_t/ \\big(\\sqrt{\\tilde{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)  \\hspace{3mm}              \\\\\n   230\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   231\u2192            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   232\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   233\u2192       \\end{aligned}\n   234\u2192\n   235\u2192    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to\n   236\u2192    `lecture notes <https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~tijmen/csc321/slides/lecture_slides_lec6.pdf>`_ by G. Hinton.\n   237\u2192    and centered version `Generating Sequences\n   238\u2192    With Recurrent Neural Networks <https://arxiv.org/pdf/1308.0850v5.pdf>`_.\n   239\u2192    The implementation here takes the square root of the gradient average before\n   240\u2192    adding epsilon (note that TensorFlow interchanges these two operations). The effective\n   241\u2192    learning rate is thus :math:`\\gamma/(\\sqrt{v} + \\epsilon)` where :math:`\\gamma`\n   242\u2192    is the scheduled learning rate and :math:`v` is the weighted moving average\n   243\u2192    of the squared gradient.\n   244\u2192    \"\"\"\n   245\u2192    + rf\"\"\"\n   246\u2192    Args:\n   247\u2192        {_params_doc}\n   248\u2192        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n   249\u2192        alpha (float, optional): smoothing constant (default: 0.99)\n   250\u2192        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   251\u2192            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n   252\u2192        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   253\u2192        momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\n   254\u2192        centered (bool, optional) : if ``True``, compute the centered RMSProp,\n   255\u2192            the gradient is normalized by an estimation of its variance\n   256\u2192        {_capturable_doc}\n   257\u2192        {_foreach_doc}\n   258\u2192        {_maximize_doc}\n   259\u2192        {_differentiable_doc}\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192    \"\"\"\n   262\u2192)\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192def _single_tensor_rmsprop(\n   266\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   267\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   268\u2192    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   269\u2192    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   270\u2192    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n   271\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   272\u2192    *,\n   273\u2192    lr: float,\n   274\u2192    alpha: float,\n   275\u2192    eps: float,\n   276\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   277\u2192    momentum: float,\n   278\u2192    centered: bool,\n   279\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   280\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   281\u2192    capturable: bool,\n   282\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   283\u2192):\n   284\u2192    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   285\u2192        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   288\u2192        step = state_steps[i]\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   291\u2192        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   292\u2192            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   293\u2192            assert (\n   294\u2192                param.device.type == step.device.type\n   295\u2192                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   296\u2192            ), (\n   297\u2192                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   298\u2192            )\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192        grad = grads[i]\n   301\u2192        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n   302\u2192        square_avg = square_avgs[i]\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192        step += 1\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192        if weight_decay != 0:\n   307\u2192            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192        is_complex_param = torch.is_complex(param)\n   310\u2192        if is_complex_param:\n   311\u2192            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   312\u2192            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   313\u2192            square_avg = torch.view_as_real(square_avg)\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192        square_avg.mul_(alpha).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - alpha)\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192        if centered:\n   318\u2192            grad_avg = grad_avgs[i]\n   319\u2192            if is_complex_param:\n   320\u2192                grad_avg = torch.view_as_real(grad_avg)\n   321\u2192            grad_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - alpha)\n   322\u2192            avg = square_avg.addcmul(grad_avg, grad_avg, value=-1).sqrt_()\n   323\u2192        else:\n   324\u2192            avg = square_avg.sqrt()\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192        if differentiable:\n   327\u2192            avg = avg.add(eps)\n   328\u2192        else:\n   329\u2192            avg = avg.add_(eps)\n   330\u2192\n   331\u2192        if momentum > 0:\n   332\u2192            buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]\n   333\u2192            if is_complex_param:\n   334\u2192                buf = torch.view_as_real(buf)\n   335\u2192            buf.mul_(momentum).addcdiv_(grad, avg)\n   336\u2192            param.add_(buf, alpha=-lr)\n   337\u2192        else:\n   338\u2192            param.addcdiv_(grad, avg, value=-lr)\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192\n   341\u2192def _multi_tensor_rmsprop(\n   342\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   343\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   344\u2192    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   345\u2192    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   346\u2192    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n   347\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   348\u2192    *,\n   349\u2192    lr: float,\n   350\u2192    alpha: float,\n   351\u2192    eps: float,\n   352\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   353\u2192    momentum: float,\n   354\u2192    centered: bool,\n   355\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   356\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   357\u2192    capturable: bool,\n   358\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   359\u2192):\n   360\u2192    if len(params) == 0:\n   361\u2192        return\n   362\u2192\n   363\u2192    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   364\u2192\n   365\u2192    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   366\u2192    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   367\u2192        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   368\u2192        assert all(\n   369\u2192            p.device.type == step.device.type\n   370\u2192            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   371\u2192            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n   372\u2192        ), (\n   373\u2192            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   374\u2192        )\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   379\u2192        [params, grads, square_avgs, grad_avgs, momentum_buffer_list, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   380\u2192    )\n   381\u2192    for (\n   382\u2192        (\n   383\u2192            grouped_params_,\n   384\u2192            grouped_grads_,\n   385\u2192            grouped_square_avgs_,\n   386\u2192            grouped_grad_avgs_,\n   387\u2192            grouped_momentum_buffer_list_,\n   388\u2192            grouped_state_steps_,\n   389\u2192        )\n   390\u2192    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n   391\u2192        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n   392\u2192        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n   393\u2192        grouped_square_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_square_avgs_)\n   394\u2192        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192        if has_complex:\n   397\u2192            state_and_grads = [grouped_grads, grouped_square_avgs]\n   398\u2192            if momentum > 0:\n   399\u2192                grouped_momentum_buffer_list = cast(\n   400\u2192                    list[Tensor], grouped_momentum_buffer_list_\n   401\u2192                )\n   402\u2192                state_and_grads.append(grouped_momentum_buffer_list)\n   403\u2192            if centered:\n   404\u2192                grouped_grad_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grad_avgs_)\n   405\u2192                state_and_grads.append(grouped_grad_avgs)\n   406\u2192            _view_as_real(grouped_params, *state_and_grads)\n   407\u2192\n   408\u2192        if maximize:\n   409\u2192            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   410\u2192\n   411\u2192        # Update steps\n   412\u2192        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   413\u2192        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   414\u2192        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   415\u2192        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   416\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(\n   417\u2192                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   418\u2192            )\n   419\u2192        else:\n   420\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   421\u2192\n   422\u2192        if weight_decay != 0:\n   423\u2192            # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n   424\u2192            if maximize:\n   425\u2192                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n   426\u2192            else:\n   427\u2192                grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   428\u2192                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   429\u2192                )\n   430\u2192\n   431\u2192        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_square_avgs, alpha)\n   432\u2192        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n   433\u2192            grouped_square_avgs, grouped_grads, grouped_grads, value=1 - alpha\n   434\u2192        )\n   435\u2192\n   436\u2192        if centered:\n   437\u2192            grouped_grad_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grad_avgs_)\n   438\u2192            torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_grad_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - alpha)\n   439\u2192            avg = torch._foreach_addcmul(\n   440\u2192                grouped_square_avgs, grouped_grad_avgs, grouped_grad_avgs, value=-1\n   441\u2192            )\n   442\u2192            torch._foreach_sqrt_(avg)\n   443\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)\n   444\u2192        else:\n   445\u2192            avg = torch._foreach_sqrt(grouped_square_avgs)\n   446\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)\n   447\u2192\n   448\u2192        if momentum > 0:\n   449\u2192            grouped_momentum_buffer_list = cast(\n   450\u2192                list[Tensor], grouped_momentum_buffer_list_\n   451\u2192            )\n   452\u2192            torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, momentum)\n   453\u2192            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, grouped_grads, avg)\n   454\u2192            # If LR is a tensor, the else branch will internally call item()\n   455\u2192            # which will cause silent incorrectness if we are capturing\n   456\u2192            if capturable and isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor):\n   457\u2192                momentum_lr = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, -lr)\n   458\u2192                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_params, momentum_lr)\n   459\u2192            else:\n   460\u2192                torch._foreach_add_(\n   461\u2192                    grouped_params, grouped_momentum_buffer_list, alpha=-lr\n   462\u2192                )\n   463\u2192        else:\n   464\u2192            # If LR is a tensor, the else branch will internally call item()\n   465\u2192            # which will cause silent incorrectness if we are capturing\n   466\u2192            if capturable and isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor):\n   467\u2192                torch._foreach_div_(avg, -lr)\n   468\u2192                torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_grads, avg)\n   469\u2192            else:\n   470\u2192                torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_grads, avg, value=-lr)\n   471\u2192\n   472\u2192\n   473\u2192@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_rmsprop)\n   474\u2192def rmsprop(\n   475\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   476\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   477\u2192    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   478\u2192    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   479\u2192    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n   480\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   481\u2192    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n   482\u2192    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n   483\u2192    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n   484\u2192    maximize: bool = False,\n   485\u2192    differentiable: bool = False,\n   486\u2192    capturable: bool = False,\n   487\u2192    has_complex: bool = False,\n   488\u2192    *,\n   489\u2192    lr: float,\n   490\u2192    alpha: float,\n   491\u2192    eps: float,\n   492\u2192    weight_decay: float,\n   493\u2192    momentum: float,\n   494\u2192    centered: bool,\n   495\u2192):\n   496\u2192    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs rmsprop algorithm computation.\n   497\u2192\n   498\u2192    See :class:`~torch.optim.RMSProp` for details.\n   499\u2192    \"\"\"\n   500\u2192    # this check is slow during compilation, so we skip it\n   501\u2192    # if it's strictly needed we can add this check back in dynamo\n   502\u2192    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and not all(\n   503\u2192        isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps\n   504\u2192    ):\n   505\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\n   506\u2192            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n   507\u2192        )\n   508\u2192\n   509\u2192    if foreach is None:\n   510\u2192        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n   511\u2192            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n   512\u2192        )\n   513\u2192\n   514\u2192    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   515\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n   516\u2192\n   517\u2192    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   518\u2192        func = _multi_tensor_rmsprop\n   519\u2192    else:\n   520\u2192        func = _single_tensor_rmsprop\n   521\u2192\n   522\u2192    func(\n   523\u2192        params,\n   524\u2192        grads,\n   525\u2192        square_avgs,\n   526\u2192        grad_avgs,\n   527\u2192        momentum_buffer_list,\n   528\u2192        state_steps,\n   529\u2192        lr=lr,\n   530\u2192        alpha=alpha,\n   531\u2192        eps=eps,\n   532\u2192        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n   533\u2192        momentum=momentum,\n   534\u2192        centered=centered,\n   535\u2192        maximize=maximize,\n   536\u2192        capturable=capturable,\n   537\u2192        differentiable=differentiable,\n   538\u2192        has_complex=has_complex,\n   539\u2192    )\n   540\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
22. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/optim/rprop.py"
}
Output
"     1\u2192# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\n     2\u2192r\"\"\"Implementation for the Resilient backpropagation.\"\"\"\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192from typing import cast, Optional, Union\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import torch\n     7\u2192from torch import Tensor\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192from .optimizer import (\n    10\u2192    _capturable_doc,\n    11\u2192    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    12\u2192    _differentiable_doc,\n    13\u2192    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    14\u2192    _foreach_doc,\n    15\u2192    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    16\u2192    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    17\u2192    _maximize_doc,\n    18\u2192    _params_doc,\n    19\u2192    _to_scalar,\n    20\u2192    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    21\u2192    _view_as_real,\n    22\u2192    Optimizer,\n    23\u2192    ParamsT,\n    24\u2192)\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192__all__ = [\"Rprop\", \"rprop\"]\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192class Rprop(Optimizer):  # noqa: D101\n    31\u2192    def __init__(\n    32\u2192        self,\n    33\u2192        params: ParamsT,\n    34\u2192        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-2,\n    35\u2192        etas: tuple[float, float] = (0.5, 1.2),\n    36\u2192        step_sizes: tuple[float, float] = (1e-6, 50),\n    37\u2192        *,\n    38\u2192        capturable: bool = False,\n    39\u2192        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    40\u2192        maximize: bool = False,\n    41\u2192        differentiable: bool = False,\n    42\u2192    ):  # noqa: D107\n    43\u2192        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n    44\u2192            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n    45\u2192        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n    46\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n    47\u2192        if not 0.0 < etas[0] < 1.0 < etas[1]:\n    48\u2192            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid eta values: {etas[0]}, {etas[1]}\")\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192        defaults = {\n    51\u2192            \"lr\": lr,\n    52\u2192            \"etas\": etas,\n    53\u2192            \"step_sizes\": step_sizes,\n    54\u2192            \"foreach\": foreach,\n    55\u2192            \"maximize\": maximize,\n    56\u2192            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n    57\u2192            \"capturable\": capturable,\n    58\u2192        }\n    59\u2192        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    def __setstate__(self, state):  # noqa: D105\n    62\u2192        super().__setstate__(state)\n    63\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n    64\u2192            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n    65\u2192            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n    66\u2192            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n    67\u2192            group.setdefault(\"capturable\", False)\n    68\u2192            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n    69\u2192                p_state = self.state.get(p, [])\n    70\u2192                if len(p_state) != 0 and not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n    71\u2192                    step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n    72\u2192                    p_state[\"step\"] = (\n    73\u2192                        torch.tensor(\n    74\u2192                            step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n    75\u2192                        )\n    76\u2192                        if group[\"capturable\"]\n    77\u2192                        else torch.tensor(step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n    78\u2192                    )\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192    def _init_group(self, group, params, grads, prevs, step_sizes, state_steps):\n    81\u2192        has_complex = False\n    82\u2192        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n    83\u2192            if p.grad is None:\n    84\u2192                continue\n    85\u2192            has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n    86\u2192            params.append(p)\n    87\u2192            grad = p.grad\n    88\u2192            if grad.is_sparse:\n    89\u2192                raise RuntimeError(\"Rprop does not support sparse gradients\")\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192            grads.append(grad)\n    92\u2192            state = self.state[p]\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192            # State initialization\n    95\u2192            if len(state) == 0:\n    96\u2192                state[\"step\"] = (\n    97\u2192                    torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n    98\u2192                    if group[\"capturable\"]\n    99\u2192                    else torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   100\u2192                )\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192                state[\"prev\"] = torch.zeros_like(p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format)\n   103\u2192                if p.dtype.is_complex:\n   104\u2192                    # Complex Number should be as if they are two independent real numbers.\n   105\u2192                    # Hence the step_size shouldn't be zero for imaginary part.\n   106\u2192                    state[\"step_size\"] = torch.full_like(\n   107\u2192                        grad, complex(group[\"lr\"], group[\"lr\"])\n   108\u2192                    )\n   109\u2192                else:\n   110\u2192                    state[\"step_size\"] = torch.full_like(grad, _to_scalar(group[\"lr\"]))\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192            prevs.append(state[\"prev\"])\n   113\u2192            step_sizes.append(state[\"step_size\"])\n   114\u2192            state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192        return has_complex\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n   119\u2192    def step(self, closure=None):\n   120\u2192        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192        Args:\n   123\u2192            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n   124\u2192                and returns the loss.\n   125\u2192        \"\"\"\n   126\u2192        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192        loss = None\n   129\u2192        if closure is not None:\n   130\u2192            with torch.enable_grad():\n   131\u2192                loss = closure()\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192        for group in self.param_groups:\n   134\u2192            params: list[Tensor] = []\n   135\u2192            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n   136\u2192            prevs: list[Tensor] = []\n   137\u2192            step_sizes: list[Tensor] = []\n   138\u2192            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192            etaminus, etaplus = group[\"etas\"]\n   141\u2192            step_size_min, step_size_max = group[\"step_sizes\"]\n   142\u2192            foreach = group[\"foreach\"]\n   143\u2192            maximize = group[\"maximize\"]\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192            has_complex = self._init_group(\n   146\u2192                group, params, grads, prevs, step_sizes, state_steps\n   147\u2192            )\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192            rprop(\n   150\u2192                params,\n   151\u2192                grads,\n   152\u2192                prevs,\n   153\u2192                step_sizes,\n   154\u2192                state_steps,\n   155\u2192                step_size_min=step_size_min,\n   156\u2192                step_size_max=step_size_max,\n   157\u2192                etaminus=etaminus,\n   158\u2192                etaplus=etaplus,\n   159\u2192                foreach=foreach,\n   160\u2192                maximize=maximize,\n   161\u2192                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n   162\u2192                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n   163\u2192                has_complex=has_complex,\n   164\u2192            )\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192        return loss\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192Rprop.__doc__ = (\n   170\u2192    r\"\"\"Implements the resilient backpropagation algorithm.\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    .. math::\n   173\u2192       \\begin{aligned}\n   174\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   175\u2192            &\\textbf{input}      : \\theta_0 \\in \\mathbf{R}^d \\text{ (params)},f(\\theta)\n   176\u2192                \\text{ (objective)},                                                             \\\\\n   177\u2192            &\\hspace{13mm}      \\eta_{+/-} \\text{ (etaplus, etaminus)}, \\Gamma_{max/min}\n   178\u2192                \\text{ (step sizes)}                                                             \\\\\n   179\u2192            &\\textbf{initialize} :   g^0_{prev} \\leftarrow 0,\n   180\u2192                \\: \\eta_0 \\leftarrow \\text{lr (learning rate)}                                   \\\\\n   181\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   182\u2192            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   183\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n   184\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm} \\textbf{for} \\text{  } i = 0, 1, \\ldots, d-1 \\: \\mathbf{do}            \\\\\n   185\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm}  \\textbf{if} \\:   g^i_{prev} g^i_t  > 0                               \\\\\n   186\u2192            &\\hspace{15mm}  \\eta^i_t \\leftarrow \\mathrm{min}(\\eta^i_{t-1} \\eta_{+},\n   187\u2192                \\Gamma_{max})                                                                    \\\\\n   188\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm}  \\textbf{else if}  \\:  g^i_{prev} g^i_t < 0                           \\\\\n   189\u2192            &\\hspace{15mm}  \\eta^i_t \\leftarrow \\mathrm{max}(\\eta^i_{t-1} \\eta_{-},\n   190\u2192                \\Gamma_{min})                                                                    \\\\\n   191\u2192            &\\hspace{15mm}  g^i_t \\leftarrow 0                                                   \\\\\n   192\u2192            &\\hspace{10mm}  \\textbf{else}  \\:                                                    \\\\\n   193\u2192            &\\hspace{15mm}  \\eta^i_t \\leftarrow \\eta^i_{t-1}                                     \\\\\n   194\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1}- \\eta_t \\mathrm{sign}(g_t)             \\\\\n   195\u2192            &\\hspace{5mm}g_{prev} \\leftarrow  g_t                                                \\\\\n   196\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   197\u2192            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   198\u2192            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   199\u2192       \\end{aligned}\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to the paper\n   202\u2192    `A Direct Adaptive Method for Faster Backpropagation Learning: The RPROP Algorithm\n   203\u2192    <http://citeseerx.ist.psu.edu/viewdoc/summary?doi=10.1.1.21.1417>`_.\"\"\"  # codespell:ignore\n   204\u2192    + rf\"\"\"\n   205\u2192\n   206\u2192    Args:\n   207\u2192        {_params_doc}\n   208\u2192        lr (float, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n   209\u2192        etas (Tuple[float, float], optional): pair of (etaminus, etaplus), that\n   210\u2192            are multiplicative increase and decrease factors\n   211\u2192            (default: (0.5, 1.2))\n   212\u2192        step_sizes (Tuple[float, float], optional): a pair of minimal and\n   213\u2192            maximal allowed step sizes (default: (1e-6, 50))\n   214\u2192        {_capturable_doc}\n   215\u2192        {_foreach_doc}\n   216\u2192        {_maximize_doc}\n   217\u2192        {_differentiable_doc}\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192    \"\"\"\n   220\u2192)\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192\n   223\u2192def _single_tensor_rprop(\n   224\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   225\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   226\u2192    prevs: list[Tensor],\n   227\u2192    step_sizes: list[Tensor],\n   228\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   229\u2192    *,\n   230\u2192    step_size_min: float,\n   231\u2192    step_size_max: float,\n   232\u2192    etaminus: float,\n   233\u2192    etaplus: float,\n   234\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   235\u2192    capturable: bool,\n   236\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   237\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   238\u2192):\n   239\u2192    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   240\u2192        grad = grads[i]\n   241\u2192        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n   242\u2192        prev = prevs[i]\n   243\u2192        step_size = step_sizes[i]\n   244\u2192        step = state_steps[i]\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   247\u2192        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   248\u2192            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   249\u2192            assert (\n   250\u2192                param.device.type == step.device.type\n   251\u2192                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   252\u2192            ), (\n   253\u2192                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   254\u2192            )\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192        step += 1\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192        if torch.is_complex(param):\n   259\u2192            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   260\u2192            prev = torch.view_as_real(prev)\n   261\u2192            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   262\u2192            step_size = torch.view_as_real(step_size)\n   263\u2192        if differentiable:\n   264\u2192            sign = grad.mul(prev.clone()).sign()\n   265\u2192        else:\n   266\u2192            sign = grad.mul(prev).sign()\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192        if capturable:\n   269\u2192            sign.copy_(torch.where(sign.gt(0), etaplus, sign))\n   270\u2192            sign.copy_(torch.where(sign.lt(0), etaminus, sign))\n   271\u2192            sign.copy_(torch.where(sign.eq(0), 1, sign))\n   272\u2192        else:\n   273\u2192            sign[sign.gt(0)] = etaplus\n   274\u2192            sign[sign.lt(0)] = etaminus\n   275\u2192            sign[sign.eq(0)] = 1\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192        # update stepsizes with step size updates\n   278\u2192        step_size.mul_(sign).clamp_(step_size_min, step_size_max)\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192        # for dir<0, dfdx=0\n   281\u2192        # for dir>=0 dfdx=dfdx\n   282\u2192        grad = grad.clone(memory_format=torch.preserve_format)\n   283\u2192        if capturable:\n   284\u2192            grad.copy_(torch.where(sign.eq(etaminus), 0, grad))\n   285\u2192        else:\n   286\u2192            grad[sign.eq(etaminus)] = 0\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192        # update parameters\n   289\u2192        param.addcmul_(grad.sign(), step_size, value=-1)\n   290\u2192        prev.copy_(grad)\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192def _multi_tensor_rprop(\n   294\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   295\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   296\u2192    prevs: list[Tensor],\n   297\u2192    step_sizes: list[Tensor],\n   298\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   299\u2192    *,\n   300\u2192    step_size_min: float,\n   301\u2192    step_size_max: float,\n   302\u2192    etaminus: float,\n   303\u2192    etaplus: float,\n   304\u2192    maximize: bool,\n   305\u2192    capturable: bool,\n   306\u2192    differentiable: bool,\n   307\u2192    has_complex: bool,\n   308\u2192):\n   309\u2192    if len(params) == 0:\n   310\u2192        return\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   315\u2192    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   316\u2192        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   317\u2192        assert all(\n   318\u2192            p.device.type == step.device.type\n   319\u2192            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   320\u2192            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n   321\u2192        ), (\n   322\u2192            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   323\u2192        )\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   326\u2192        [params, grads, prevs, step_sizes, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   327\u2192    )\n   328\u2192    for (\n   329\u2192        grouped_params_,\n   330\u2192        grouped_grads_,\n   331\u2192        grouped_prevs_,\n   332\u2192        grouped_step_sizes_,\n   333\u2192        grouped_state_steps_,\n   334\u2192    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n   335\u2192        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n   336\u2192        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n   337\u2192        grouped_prevs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_prevs_)\n   338\u2192        grouped_step_sizes = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_step_sizes_)\n   339\u2192        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n   340\u2192\n   341\u2192        # Update steps\n   342\u2192        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   343\u2192        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   344\u2192        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   345\u2192        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   346\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(\n   347\u2192                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   348\u2192            )\n   349\u2192        else:\n   350\u2192            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   351\u2192\n   352\u2192        # Handle complex params\n   353\u2192        if has_complex:\n   354\u2192            _view_as_real(\n   355\u2192                grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_prevs, grouped_step_sizes\n   356\u2192            )\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192        signs = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_grads, grouped_prevs)\n   359\u2192        if maximize:\n   360\u2192            torch._foreach_neg_(signs)\n   361\u2192\n   362\u2192        # At the end of the step, grouped_prevs will contain the current grads, so we reuse\n   363\u2192        # grouped_prevs memory instead of creating a new buffer, but, for clarity, we reassign\n   364\u2192        # to keep referring to the buffer as grouped_grads.\n   365\u2192        torch._foreach_copy_(grouped_prevs, grouped_grads)\n   366\u2192        if maximize:\n   367\u2192            torch._foreach_neg_(grouped_prevs)\n   368\u2192        grouped_grads = grouped_prevs\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192        torch._foreach_sign_(signs)\n   371\u2192        if capturable:\n   372\u2192            for sign in signs:\n   373\u2192                sign.copy_(torch.where(sign.gt(0), etaplus, sign))\n   374\u2192                sign.copy_(torch.where(sign.lt(0), etaminus, sign))\n   375\u2192                sign.copy_(torch.where(sign.eq(0), 1, sign))\n   376\u2192        else:\n   377\u2192            for sign in signs:\n   378\u2192                sign[sign.gt(0)] = etaplus\n   379\u2192                sign[sign.lt(0)] = etaminus\n   380\u2192                sign[sign.eq(0)] = 1\n   381\u2192\n   382\u2192        # update stepsizes with step size updates\n   383\u2192        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_step_sizes, signs)\n   384\u2192        for step_size in grouped_step_sizes:\n   385\u2192            step_size.clamp_(step_size_min, step_size_max)\n   386\u2192\n   387\u2192        # for dir<0, dfdx=0\n   388\u2192        # for dir>=0 dfdx=dfdx\n   389\u2192        grouped_grads = list(grouped_grads)\n   390\u2192        for i in range(len(grouped_grads)):\n   391\u2192            grouped_grads[i].copy_(\n   392\u2192                torch.where(signs[i].eq(etaminus), 0, grouped_grads[i])\n   393\u2192            )\n   394\u2192\n   395\u2192        # explicitly del signs as it's not used after here to save memory\n   396\u2192        del signs\n   397\u2192\n   398\u2192        # update parameters\n   399\u2192        grad_signs = [grad.sign() for grad in grouped_grads]\n   400\u2192        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n   401\u2192            grouped_params, grad_signs, grouped_step_sizes, value=-1\n   402\u2192        )\n   403\u2192\n   404\u2192        # Logically, you may expect grouped_prevs to get updated to grouped_grads, but that's\n   405\u2192        # basically already happened since we've been using grouped_prevs' memory to store\n   406\u2192        # updated grouped_grads!\n   407\u2192\n   408\u2192\n   409\u2192@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_rprop)\n   410\u2192def rprop(\n   411\u2192    params: list[Tensor],\n   412\u2192    grads: list[Tensor],\n   413\u2192    prevs: list[Tensor],\n   414\u2192    step_sizes: list[Tensor],\n   415\u2192    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   416\u2192    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n   417\u2192    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n   418\u2192    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n   419\u2192    capturable: bool = False,\n   420\u2192    maximize: bool = False,\n   421\u2192    differentiable: bool = False,\n   422\u2192    has_complex: bool = False,\n   423\u2192    *,\n   424\u2192    step_size_min: float,\n   425\u2192    step_size_max: float,\n   426\u2192    etaminus: float,\n   427\u2192    etaplus: float,\n   428\u2192):\n   429\u2192    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs rprop algorithm computation.\n   430\u2192\n   431\u2192    See :class:`~torch.optim.Rprop` for details.\n   432\u2192    \"\"\"\n   433\u2192    # this check is slow during compilation, so we skip it\n   434\u2192    # if it's strictly needed we can add this check back in dynamo\n   435\u2192    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and not all(\n   436\u2192        isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps\n   437\u2192    ):\n   438\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\n   439\u2192            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n   440\u2192        )\n   441\u2192\n   442\u2192    if foreach is None:\n   443\u2192        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n   444\u2192            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n   445\u2192        )\n   446\u2192\n   447\u2192    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   448\u2192        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n   449\u2192\n   450\u2192    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   451\u2192        func = _multi_tensor_rprop\n   452\u2192    else:\n   453\u2192        func = _single_tensor_rprop\n   454\u2192\n   455\u2192    func(\n   456\u2192        params,\n   457\u2192        grads,\n   458\u2192        prevs,\n   459\u2192        step_sizes,\n   460\u2192        state_steps,\n   461\u2192        step_size_min=step_size_min,\n   462\u2192        step_size_max=step_size_max,\n   463\u2192        etaminus=etaminus,\n   464\u2192        etaplus=etaplus,\n   465\u2192        capturable=capturable,\n   466\u2192        maximize=maximize,\n   467\u2192        differentiable=differentiable,\n   468\u2192        has_complex=has_complex,\n   469\u2192    )\n   470\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
