  "file": {
    "content": "# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\nr\"\"\"Implementation for the RMSprop algorithm.\"\"\"\n\nfrom typing import cast, Optional, Union\n\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\n\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _capturable_doc,\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)\n\n\n__all__ = [\"RMSprop\", \"rmsprop\"]\n\n\nclass RMSprop(Optimizer):  # noqa: D101\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        params: ParamsT,\n        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-2,\n        alpha: float = 0.99,\n        eps: float = 1e-8,\n        weight_decay: float = 0,\n        momentum: float = 0,\n        centered: bool = False,\n        capturable: bool = False,\n        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n        maximize: bool = False,\n        differentiable: bool = False,\n    ):  # noqa: D107\n        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= eps:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid epsilon value: {eps}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= momentum:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid momentum value: {momentum}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= alpha:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid alpha value: {alpha}\")\n\n        defaults = {\n            \"lr\": lr,\n            \"momentum\": momentum,\n            \"alpha\": alpha,\n            \"eps\": eps,\n            \"centered\": centered,\n            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n            \"capturable\": capturable,\n            \"foreach\": foreach,\n            \"maximize\": maximize,\n            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n        }\n        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n\n    def __setstate__(self, state):  # noqa: D105\n        super().__setstate__(state)\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            group.setdefault(\"momentum\", 0)\n            group.setdefault(\"centered\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"capturable\", False)\n            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n                p_state = self.state.get(p, [])\n                if len(p_state) != 0 and not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n                    step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n                    p_state[\"step\"] = (\n                        torch.tensor(\n                            step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n                        )\n                        if group[\"capturable\"]\n                        else torch.tensor(step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n                    )\n\n    def _init_group(\n        self,\n        group,\n        params_with_grad,\n        grads,\n        square_avgs,\n        momentum_buffer_list,\n        grad_avgs,",

  "file": {
    "content": "def _multi_tensor_rmsprop(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    alpha: float,\n    eps: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    centered: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, square_avgs, grad_avgs, momentum_buffer_list, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        (\n            grouped_params_,\n            grouped_grads_,\n            grouped_square_avgs_,\n            grouped_grad_avgs_,\n            grouped_momentum_buffer_list_,\n            grouped_state_steps_,\n        )\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n        grouped_square_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_square_avgs_)\n        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n\n        if has_complex:\n            state_and_grads = [grouped_grads, grouped_square_avgs]\n            if momentum > 0:\n                grouped_momentum_buffer_list = cast(\n                    list[Tensor], grouped_momentum_buffer_list_\n                )\n                state_and_grads.append(grouped_momentum_buffer_list)\n            if centered:\n                grouped_grad_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grad_avgs_)\n                state_and_grads.append(grouped_grad_avgs)\n            _view_as_real(grouped_params, *state_and_grads)\n\n        if maximize:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_square_avgs, alpha)\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n            grouped_square_avgs, grouped_grads, grouped_grads, value=1 - alpha\n        )\n\n        if centered:\n            grouped_grad_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grad_avgs_)\n            torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_grad_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - alpha)\n            avg = torch._foreach_addcmul(\n                grouped_square_avgs, grouped_grad_avgs, grouped_grad_avgs, value=-1\n            )\n            torch._foreach_sqrt_(avg)\n            torch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)\n        else:\n            avg = torch._foreach_sqrt(grouped_square_avgs)\n            torch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)\n\n        if momentum > 0:\n            grouped_momentum_buffer_list = cast(\n                list[Tensor], grouped_momentum_buffer_list_\n            )\n            torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, momentum)\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, grouped_grads, avg)\n            # If LR is a tensor, the else branch will internally call item()\n            # which will cause silent incorrectness if we are capturing\n            if capturable and isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor):\n                momentum_lr = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, -lr)\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_params, momentum_lr)\n            else:\n                torch._foreach_add_(\n                    grouped_params, grouped_momentum_buffer_list, alpha=-lr\n                )\n        else:\n            # If LR is a tensor, the else branch will internally call item()\n            # which will cause silent incorrectness if we are capturing\n            if capturable and isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor):\n                torch._foreach_div_(avg, -lr)\n                torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_grads, avg)\n            else:\n                torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_grads, avg, value=-lr)\n\n\n@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_rmsprop)\ndef rmsprop(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    capturable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    alpha: float,\n    eps: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    centered: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs rmsprop algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.RMSProp` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    # this check is slow during compilation, so we skip it\n    # if it's strictly needed we can add this check back in dynamo\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and not all(\n        isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps\n    ):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    if foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n\n    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_rmsprop\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_rmsprop\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        square_avgs,\n        grad_avgs,\n        momentum_buffer_list,\n        state_steps,\n        lr=lr,\n        alpha=alpha,\n        eps=eps,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        momentum=momentum,\n        centered=centered,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        capturable=capturable,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n    )\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/optim/rmsprop.py",
    "numLines": 200,
    "startLine": 341,
    "totalLines": 540

  "filePath": "/workspace/torch/optim/rmsprop.py",
  "newString": "from ._foreach import (\n    apply_maximize,\n    apply_weight_decay,\n    check_empty_params,\n    group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype,\n    handle_capturable_devices,\n    handle_step_updates,\n)\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _capturable_doc,\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)",
  "oldString": "from .optimizer import (\n    _capturable_doc,\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)",
  "originalFile": "# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\nr\"\"\"Implementation for the RMSprop algorithm.\"\"\"\n\nfrom typing import cast, Optional, Union\n\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\n\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _capturable_doc,\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)\n\n\n__all__ = [\"RMSprop\", \"rmsprop\"]\n\n\nclass RMSprop(Optimizer):  # noqa: D101\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        params: ParamsT,\n        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-2,\n        alpha: float = 0.99,\n        eps: float = 1e-8,\n        weight_decay: float = 0,\n        momentum: float = 0,\n        centered: bool = False,\n        capturable: bool = False,\n        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n        maximize: bool = False,\n        differentiable: bool = False,\n    ):  # noqa: D107\n        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= eps:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid epsilon value: {eps}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= momentum:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid momentum value: {momentum}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= alpha:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid alpha value: {alpha}\")\n\n        defaults = {\n            \"lr\": lr,\n            \"momentum\": momentum,\n            \"alpha\": alpha,\n            \"eps\": eps,\n            \"centered\": centered,\n            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n            \"capturable\": capturable,\n            \"foreach\": foreach,\n            \"maximize\": maximize,\n            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n        }\n        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n\n    def __setstate__(self, state):  # noqa: D105\n        super().__setstate__(state)\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            group.setdefault(\"momentum\", 0)\n            group.setdefault(\"centered\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"capturable\", False)\n            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n                p_state = self.state.get(p, [])\n                if len(p_state) != 0 and not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n                    step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n                    p_state[\"step\"] = (\n                        torch.tensor(\n                            step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n                        )\n                        if group[\"capturable\"]\n                        else torch.tensor(step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n                    )\n\n    def _init_group(\n        self,\n        group,\n        params_with_grad,\n        grads,\n        square_avgs,\n        momentum_buffer_list,\n        grad_avgs,\n        state_steps,\n    ):\n        has_complex = False\n        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n            if p.grad is None:\n                continue\n            has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n            params_with_grad.append(p)\n\n            if p.grad.is_sparse:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"RMSprop does not support sparse gradients\")\n            grads.append(p.grad)\n\n            state = self.state[p]\n\n            # State initialization\n            if len(state) == 0:\n                state[\"step\"] = (\n                    torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n                    if group[\"capturable\"]\n                    else torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n                )\n                state[\"square_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                    p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                )\n                if group[\"momentum\"] > 0:\n                    state[\"momentum_buffer\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                    )\n                if group[\"centered\"]:\n                    state[\"grad_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                    )\n            square_avgs.append(state[\"square_avg\"])\n            state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n\n            if group[\"momentum\"] > 0:\n                momentum_buffer_list.append(state[\"momentum_buffer\"])\n            if group[\"centered\"]:\n                grad_avgs.append(state[\"grad_avg\"])\n\n        return has_complex\n\n    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n    def step(self, closure=None):\n        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n\n        Args:\n            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n                and returns the loss.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n\n        loss = None\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                loss = closure()\n\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n            square_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n            grad_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n            momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor] = []\n            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n\n            has_complex = self._init_group(\n                group,\n                params_with_grad,\n                grads,\n                square_avgs,\n                momentum_buffer_list,\n                grad_avgs,\n                state_steps,\n            )\n\n            rmsprop(\n                params_with_grad,\n                grads,\n                square_avgs,\n                grad_avgs,\n                momentum_buffer_list,\n                state_steps,\n                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n                alpha=group[\"alpha\"],\n                eps=group[\"eps\"],\n                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n                momentum=group[\"momentum\"],\n                centered=group[\"centered\"],\n                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n            )\n\n        return loss\n\n\nRMSprop.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements RMSprop algorithm.\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\alpha \\text{ (alpha)}, \\: \\gamma \\text{ (lr)},\n                \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}                   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{13mm}   \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},\\: \\mu \\text{ (momentum)},\n                \\: centered, \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                                       \\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} : v_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (square average)}, \\:\n                \\textbf{b}_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (buffer)}, \\: g^{ave}_0 \\leftarrow 0     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                                    \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\alpha v_{t-1} + (1 - \\alpha) g^2_t\n                \\hspace{8mm}                                                                     \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} \\tilde{v_t} \\leftarrow v_t                                             \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: centered                                                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g^{ave}_t \\leftarrow g^{ave}_{t-1} \\alpha + (1-\\alpha) g_t            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} \\tilde{v_t} \\leftarrow \\tilde{v_t} -  \\big(g^{ave}_{t} \\big)^2        \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\mu > 0                                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} \\textbf{b}_t\\leftarrow \\mu \\textbf{b}_{t-1} +\n                g_t/ \\big(\\sqrt{\\tilde{v_t}} +  \\epsilon \\big)                                   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\textbf{b}_t                \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} else                                                                   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\theta_t      \\leftarrow   \\theta_{t-1} -\n                \\gamma  g_t/ \\big(\\sqrt{\\tilde{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)  \\hspace{3mm}              \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to\n    `lecture notes <https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~tijmen/csc321/slides/lecture_slides_lec6.pdf>`_ by G. Hinton.\n    and centered version `Generating Sequences\n    With Recurrent Neural Networks <https://arxiv.org/pdf/1308.0850v5.pdf>`_.\n    The implementation here takes the square root of the gradient average before\n    adding epsilon (note that TensorFlow interchanges these two operations). The effective\n    learning rate is thus :math:`\\gamma/(\\sqrt{v} + \\epsilon)` where :math:`\\gamma`\n    is the scheduled learning rate and :math:`v` is the weighted moving average\n    of the squared gradient.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n        alpha (float, optional): smoothing constant (default: 0.99)\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\n        centered (bool, optional) : if ``True``, compute the centered RMSProp,\n            the gradient is normalized by an estimation of its variance\n        {_capturable_doc}\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_rmsprop(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    alpha: float,\n    eps: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    centered: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n        step = state_steps[i]\n\n        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n            assert (\n                param.device.type == step.device.type\n                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            ), (\n                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n            )\n\n        grad = grads[i]\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n        square_avg = square_avgs[i]\n\n        step += 1\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        is_complex_param = torch.is_complex(param)\n        if is_complex_param:\n            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n            square_avg = torch.view_as_real(square_avg)\n\n        square_avg.mul_(alpha).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - alpha)\n\n        if centered:\n            grad_avg = grad_avgs[i]\n            if is_complex_param:\n                grad_avg = torch.view_as_real(grad_avg)\n            grad_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - alpha)\n            avg = square_avg.addcmul(grad_avg, grad_avg, value=-1).sqrt_()\n        else:\n            avg = square_avg.sqrt()\n\n        if differentiable:\n            avg = avg.add(eps)\n        else:\n            avg = avg.add_(eps)\n\n        if momentum > 0:\n            buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]\n            if is_complex_param:\n                buf = torch.view_as_real(buf)\n            buf.mul_(momentum).addcdiv_(grad, avg)\n            param.add_(buf, alpha=-lr)\n        else:\n            param.addcdiv_(grad, avg, value=-lr)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_rmsprop(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    alpha: float,\n    eps: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    centered: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, square_avgs, grad_avgs, momentum_buffer_list, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        (\n            grouped_params_,\n            grouped_grads_,\n            grouped_square_avgs_,\n            grouped_grad_avgs_,\n            grouped_momentum_buffer_list_,\n            grouped_state_steps_,\n        )\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n        grouped_square_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_square_avgs_)\n        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n\n        if has_complex:\n            state_and_grads = [grouped_grads, grouped_square_avgs]\n            if momentum > 0:\n                grouped_momentum_buffer_list = cast(\n                    list[Tensor], grouped_momentum_buffer_list_\n                )\n                state_and_grads.append(grouped_momentum_buffer_list)\n            if centered:\n                grouped_grad_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grad_avgs_)\n                state_and_grads.append(grouped_grad_avgs)\n            _view_as_real(grouped_params, *state_and_grads)\n\n        if maximize:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_square_avgs, alpha)\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n            grouped_square_avgs, grouped_grads, grouped_grads, value=1 - alpha\n        )\n\n        if centered:\n            grouped_grad_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grad_avgs_)\n            torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_grad_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - alpha)\n            avg = torch._foreach_addcmul(\n                grouped_square_avgs, grouped_grad_avgs, grouped_grad_avgs, value=-1\n            )\n            torch._foreach_sqrt_(avg)\n            torch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)\n        else:\n            avg = torch._foreach_sqrt(grouped_square_avgs)\n            torch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)\n\n        if momentum > 0:\n            grouped_momentum_buffer_list = cast(\n                list[Tensor], grouped_momentum_buffer_list_\n            )\n            torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, momentum)\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, grouped_grads, avg)\n            # If LR is a tensor, the else branch will internally call item()\n            # which will cause silent incorrectness if we are capturing\n            if capturable and isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor):\n                momentum_lr = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, -lr)\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_params, momentum_lr)\n            else:\n                torch._foreach_add_(\n                    grouped_params, grouped_momentum_buffer_list, alpha=-lr\n                )\n        else:\n            # If LR is a tensor, the else branch will internally call item()\n            # which will cause silent incorrectness if we are capturing\n            if capturable and isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor):\n                torch._foreach_div_(avg, -lr)\n                torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_grads, avg)\n            else:\n                torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_grads, avg, value=-lr)\n\n\n@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_rmsprop)\ndef rmsprop(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    capturable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    alpha: float,\n    eps: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    centered: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs rmsprop algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.RMSProp` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    # this check is slow during compilation, so we skip it\n    # if it's strictly needed we can add this check back in dynamo\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and not all(\n        isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps\n    ):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    if foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n\n    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_rmsprop\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_rmsprop\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        square_avgs,\n        grad_avgs,\n        momentum_buffer_list,\n        state_steps,\n        lr=lr,\n        alpha=alpha,\n        eps=eps,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        momentum=momentum,\n        centered=centered,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        capturable=capturable,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n    )\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " import torch",
        " from torch import Tensor",
        " ",
        "+from ._foreach import (",

        "+    apply_weight_decay,",
        "+    check_empty_params,",
        "+    group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype,",
        "+    handle_capturable_devices,",

type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        (\n            grouped_params_,\n            grouped_grads_,\n            grouped_square_avgs_,\n            grouped_grad_avgs_,\n            grouped_momentum_buffer_list_,\n            grouped_state_steps_,\n        )\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n        grouped_square_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_square_avgs_)\n        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n\n        if has_complex:\n            state_and_grads = [grouped_grads, grouped_square_avgs]\n            if momentum > 0:\n                grouped_momentum_buffer_list = cast(\n                    list[Tensor], grouped_momentum_buffer_list_\n                )\n                state_and_grads.append(grouped_momentum_buffer_list)\n            if centered:\n                grouped_grad_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grad_avgs_)\n                state_and_grads.append(grouped_grad_avgs)\n            _view_as_real(grouped_params, *state_and_grads)\n\n        grouped_grads = apply_maximize(grouped_grads, maximize)\n\n        # Update steps\n        handle_step_updates(grouped_state_steps)\n\n        grouped_grads = apply_weight_decay(\n            grouped_grads, grouped_params, weight_decay, maximize\n        )",

  "oldString": "def _multi_tensor_rmsprop(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    alpha: float,\n    eps: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    centered: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, square_avgs, grad_avgs, momentum_buffer_list, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        (\n            grouped_params_,\n            grouped_grads_,\n            grouped_square_avgs_,\n            grouped_grad_avgs_,\n            grouped_momentum_buffer_list_,\n            grouped_state_steps_,\n        )\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n        grouped_square_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_square_avgs_)\n        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n\n        if has_complex:\n            state_and_grads = [grouped_grads, grouped_square_avgs]\n            if momentum > 0:\n                grouped_momentum_buffer_list = cast(\n                    list[Tensor], grouped_momentum_buffer_list_\n                )\n                state_and_grads.append(grouped_momentum_buffer_list)\n            if centered:\n                grouped_grad_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grad_avgs_)\n                state_and_grads.append(grouped_grad_avgs)\n            _view_as_real(grouped_params, *state_and_grads)\n\n        if maximize:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )",
  "originalFile": "# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\nr\"\"\"Implementation for the RMSprop algorithm.\"\"\"\n\nfrom typing import cast, Optional, Union\n\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\n\nfrom ._foreach import (\n    apply_maximize,\n    apply_weight_decay,\n    check_empty_params,\n    group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype,\n    handle_capturable_devices,\n    handle_step_updates,\n)\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _capturable_doc,\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)\n\n\n__all__ = [\"RMSprop\", \"rmsprop\"]\n\n\nclass RMSprop(Optimizer):  # noqa: D101\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        params: ParamsT,\n        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-2,\n        alpha: float = 0.99,\n        eps: float = 1e-8,\n        weight_decay: float = 0,\n        momentum: float = 0,\n        centered: bool = False,\n        capturable: bool = False,\n        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n        maximize: bool = False,\n        differentiable: bool = False,\n    ):  # noqa: D107\n        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= eps:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid epsilon value: {eps}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= momentum:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid momentum value: {momentum}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= alpha:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid alpha value: {alpha}\")\n\n        defaults = {\n            \"lr\": lr,\n            \"momentum\": momentum,\n            \"alpha\": alpha,\n            \"eps\": eps,\n            \"centered\": centered,\n            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n            \"capturable\": capturable,\n            \"foreach\": foreach,\n            \"maximize\": maximize,\n            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n        }\n        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n\n    def __setstate__(self, state):  # noqa: D105\n        super().__setstate__(state)\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            group.setdefault(\"momentum\", 0)\n            group.setdefault(\"centered\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"capturable\", False)\n            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n                p_state = self.state.get(p, [])\n                if len(p_state) != 0 and not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n                    step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n                    p_state[\"step\"] = (\n                        torch.tensor(\n                            step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n                        )\n                        if group[\"capturable\"]\n                        else torch.tensor(step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n                    )\n\n    def _init_group(\n        self,\n        group,\n        params_with_grad,\n        grads,\n        square_avgs,\n        momentum_buffer_list,\n        grad_avgs,\n        state_steps,\n    ):\n        has_complex = False\n        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n            if p.grad is None:\n                continue\n            has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n            params_with_grad.append(p)\n\n            if p.grad.is_sparse:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"RMSprop does not support sparse gradients\")\n            grads.append(p.grad)\n\n            state = self.state[p]\n\n            # State initialization\n            if len(state) == 0:\n                state[\"step\"] = (\n                    torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n                    if group[\"capturable\"]\n                    else torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n                )\n                state[\"square_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                    p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                )\n                if group[\"momentum\"] > 0:\n                    state[\"momentum_buffer\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                    )\n                if group[\"centered\"]:\n                    state[\"grad_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                    )\n            square_avgs.append(state[\"square_avg\"])\n            state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n\n            if group[\"momentum\"] > 0:\n                momentum_buffer_list.append(state[\"momentum_buffer\"])\n            if group[\"centered\"]:\n                grad_avgs.append(state[\"grad_avg\"])\n\n        return has_complex\n\n    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n    def step(self, closure=None):\n        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n\n        Args:\n            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n                and returns the loss.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n\n        loss = None\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                loss = closure()\n\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n            square_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n            grad_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n            momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor] = []\n            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n\n            has_complex = self._init_group(\n                group,\n                params_with_grad,\n                grads,\n                square_avgs,\n                momentum_buffer_list,\n                grad_avgs,\n                state_steps,\n            )\n\n            rmsprop(\n                params_with_grad,\n                grads,\n                square_avgs,\n                grad_avgs,\n                momentum_buffer_list,\n                state_steps,\n                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n                alpha=group[\"alpha\"],\n                eps=group[\"eps\"],\n                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n                momentum=group[\"momentum\"],\n                centered=group[\"centered\"],\n                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n            )\n\n        return loss\n\n\nRMSprop.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements RMSprop algorithm.\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\alpha \\text{ (alpha)}, \\: \\gamma \\text{ (lr)},\n                \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}                   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{13mm}   \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},\\: \\mu \\text{ (momentum)},\n                \\: centered, \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                                       \\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} : v_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (square average)}, \\:\n                \\textbf{b}_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (buffer)}, \\: g^{ave}_0 \\leftarrow 0     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                                    \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\alpha v_{t-1} + (1 - \\alpha) g^2_t\n                \\hspace{8mm}                                                                     \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} \\tilde{v_t} \\leftarrow v_t                                             \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: centered                                                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g^{ave}_t \\leftarrow g^{ave}_{t-1} \\alpha + (1-\\alpha) g_t            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} \\tilde{v_t} \\leftarrow \\tilde{v_t} -  \\big(g^{ave}_{t} \\big)^2        \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\mu > 0                                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} \\textbf{b}_t\\leftarrow \\mu \\textbf{b}_{t-1} +\n                g_t/ \\big(\\sqrt{\\tilde{v_t}} +  \\epsilon \\big)                                   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\textbf{b}_t                \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} else                                                                   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\theta_t      \\leftarrow   \\theta_{t-1} -\n                \\gamma  g_t/ \\big(\\sqrt{\\tilde{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)  \\hspace{3mm}              \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to\n    `lecture notes <https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~tijmen/csc321/slides/lecture_slides_lec6.pdf>`_ by G. Hinton.\n    and centered version `Generating Sequences\n    With Recurrent Neural Networks <https://arxiv.org/pdf/1308.0850v5.pdf>`_.\n    The implementation here takes the square root of the gradient average before\n    adding epsilon (note that TensorFlow interchanges these two operations). The effective\n    learning rate is thus :math:`\\gamma/(\\sqrt{v} + \\epsilon)` where :math:`\\gamma`\n    is the scheduled learning rate and :math:`v` is the weighted moving average\n    of the squared gradient.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n        alpha (float, optional): smoothing constant (default: 0.99)\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\n        centered (bool, optional) : if ``True``, compute the centered RMSProp,\n            the gradient is normalized by an estimation of its variance\n        {_capturable_doc}\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_rmsprop(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    alpha: float,\n    eps: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    centered: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n        step = state_steps[i]\n\n        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n            assert (\n                param.device.type == step.device.type\n                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            ), (\n                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n            )\n\n        grad = grads[i]\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n        square_avg = square_avgs[i]\n\n        step += 1\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        is_complex_param = torch.is_complex(param)\n        if is_complex_param:\n            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n            square_avg = torch.view_as_real(square_avg)\n\n        square_avg.mul_(alpha).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - alpha)\n\n        if centered:\n            grad_avg = grad_avgs[i]\n            if is_complex_param:\n                grad_avg = torch.view_as_real(grad_avg)\n            grad_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - alpha)\n            avg = square_avg.addcmul(grad_avg, grad_avg, value=-1).sqrt_()\n        else:\n            avg = square_avg.sqrt()\n\n        if differentiable:\n            avg = avg.add(eps)\n        else:\n            avg = avg.add_(eps)\n\n        if momentum > 0:\n            buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]\n            if is_complex_param:\n                buf = torch.view_as_real(buf)\n            buf.mul_(momentum).addcdiv_(grad, avg)\n            param.add_(buf, alpha=-lr)\n        else:\n            param.addcdiv_(grad, avg, value=-lr)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_rmsprop(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    alpha: float,\n    eps: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    centered: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, square_avgs, grad_avgs, momentum_buffer_list, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        (\n            grouped_params_,\n            grouped_grads_,\n            grouped_square_avgs_,\n            grouped_grad_avgs_,\n            grouped_momentum_buffer_list_,\n            grouped_state_steps_,\n        )\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n        grouped_square_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_square_avgs_)\n        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n\n        if has_complex:\n            state_and_grads = [grouped_grads, grouped_square_avgs]\n            if momentum > 0:\n                grouped_momentum_buffer_list = cast(\n                    list[Tensor], grouped_momentum_buffer_list_\n                )\n                state_and_grads.append(grouped_momentum_buffer_list)\n            if centered:\n                grouped_grad_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grad_avgs_)\n                state_and_grads.append(grouped_grad_avgs)\n            _view_as_real(grouped_params, *state_and_grads)\n\n        if maximize:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_square_avgs, alpha)\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n            grouped_square_avgs, grouped_grads, grouped_grads, value=1 - alpha\n        )\n\n        if centered:\n            grouped_grad_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grad_avgs_)\n            torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_grad_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - alpha)\n            avg = torch._foreach_addcmul(\n                grouped_square_avgs, grouped_grad_avgs, grouped_grad_avgs, value=-1\n            )\n            torch._foreach_sqrt_(avg)\n            torch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)\n        else:\n            avg = torch._foreach_sqrt(grouped_square_avgs)\n            torch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)\n\n        if momentum > 0:\n            grouped_momentum_buffer_list = cast(\n                list[Tensor], grouped_momentum_buffer_list_\n            )\n            torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, momentum)\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, grouped_grads, avg)\n            # If LR is a tensor, the else branch will internally call item()\n            # which will cause silent incorrectness if we are capturing\n            if capturable and isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor):\n                momentum_lr = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, -lr)\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_params, momentum_lr)\n            else:\n                torch._foreach_add_(\n                    grouped_params, grouped_momentum_buffer_list, alpha=-lr\n                )\n        else:\n            # If LR is a tensor, the else branch will internally call item()\n            # which will cause silent incorrectness if we are capturing\n            if capturable and isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor):\n                torch._foreach_div_(avg, -lr)\n                torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_grads, avg)\n            else:\n                torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_grads, avg, value=-lr)\n\n\n@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_rmsprop)\ndef rmsprop(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    capturable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    alpha: float,\n    eps: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    centered: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs rmsprop algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.RMSProp` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    # this check is slow during compilation, so we skip it\n    # if it's strictly needed we can add this check back in dynamo\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and not all(\n        isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps\n    ):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    if foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n\n    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_rmsprop\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_rmsprop\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        square_avgs,\n        grad_avgs,\n        momentum_buffer_list,\n        state_steps,\n        lr=lr,\n        alpha=alpha,\n        eps=eps,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        momentum=momentum,\n        centered=centered,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        capturable=capturable,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n    )\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

    {
      "lines": [
        "                 state_and_grads.append(grouped_grad_avgs)",
        "             _view_as_real(grouped_params, *state_and_grads)",
        " ",
        "-        if maximize:",
        "-            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]",
        "+        grouped_grads = apply_maximize(grouped_grads, maximize)",

        "-        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over",
        "-        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just",
        "-        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.",
        "-        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:",
        "-            torch._foreach_add_(",
        "-                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0",
        "-            )",
        "-        else:",
        "-            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)",
        "+        handle_step_updates(grouped_state_steps)",
        " ",
        "-        if weight_decay != 0:",
        "-            # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize",
        "-            if maximize:",
        "-                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)",
        "-            else:",
        "-                grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]",
        "-                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay",
        "-                )",
        "+        grouped_grads = apply_weight_decay(",
        "+            grouped_grads, grouped_params, weight_decay, maximize",
        "+        )",
        " ",
        "         torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_square_avgs, alpha)",
        "         torch._foreach_addcmul_("
      ],
      "newLines": 14,
      "newStart": 404,
      "oldLines": 28,
      "oldStart": 413
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

{
  "file": {
    "content": "def _multi_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    # Foreach functions will throw errors if given empty lists\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensorlists = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_state_sums_,\n        device_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensorlists.values():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        if device_has_sparse_grad:\n            _single_tensor_adagrad(\n                device_params,\n                device_grads,\n                device_state_sums,\n                device_state_steps,\n                lr=lr,\n                weight_decay=weight_decay,\n                lr_decay=lr_decay,\n                eps=eps,\n                has_sparse_grad=True,\n                maximize=maximize,\n                differentiable=differentiable,\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n                grad_scale=grad_scale,\n                found_inf=found_inf,\n            )\n            continue\n\n        # Handle complex parameters\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(device_params, device_grads, device_state_sums)\n\n        if maximize:\n            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        minus_clr = [\n            -lr / (1 + (_get_value(step) - 1) * lr_decay) for step in device_state_steps\n        ]\n\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(device_state_sums, device_grads, device_grads, value=1)\n\n        std = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_state_sums)\n        torch._foreach_add_(std, eps)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0 or maximize:\n            # Again, reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated\n            torch._foreach_mul_(device_grads, minus_clr)\n            numerator = device_grads\n        else:\n            numerator = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, minus_clr)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        torch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, numerator, std)\n\n\ndef _fused_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n) -> None:\n    if not params:\n        return\n    if has_sparse_grad or has_complex:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support sparse grad or complex param\")\n\n    if differentiable:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"adagrad with fused=True does not support differentiable=True\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grad_scale_dict = (\n        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else None\n    )\n    found_inf_dict = {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else None\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (device, _), (\n        (\n            device_params_,\n            device_grads_,",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/optim/adagrad.py",
