  "newString": "from ._foreach import (\n    apply_maximize,\n    apply_weight_decay,\n    check_empty_params,\n    group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype,\n    handle_step_updates,\n)\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _device_dtype_check_for_fused,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    _get_value,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)",
  "oldString": "from .optimizer import (\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _device_dtype_check_for_fused,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    _get_value,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)",
  "originalFile": "# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\nfrom typing import cast, Optional, Union\n\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\n\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _device_dtype_check_for_fused,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    _get_value,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)\n\n\n__all__ = [\"Adagrad\", \"adagrad\"]\n\n\nclass Adagrad(Optimizer):\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        params: ParamsT,\n        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-2,\n        lr_decay: float = 0,\n        weight_decay: float = 0,\n        initial_accumulator_value: float = 0,\n        eps: float = 1e-10,\n        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n        *,\n        maximize: bool = False,\n        differentiable: bool = False,\n        fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    ):\n        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= lr_decay:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid lr_decay value: {lr_decay}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= initial_accumulator_value:\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"Invalid initial_accumulator_value value: {initial_accumulator_value}\"\n            )\n        if not 0.0 <= eps:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid epsilon value: {eps}\")\n\n        defaults = {\n            \"lr\": lr,\n            \"lr_decay\": lr_decay,\n            \"eps\": eps,\n            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n            \"initial_accumulator_value\": initial_accumulator_value,\n            \"foreach\": foreach,\n            \"maximize\": maximize,\n            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n            \"fused\": fused,\n        }\n        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n\n        if fused:\n            if differentiable:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support `differentiable`\")\n            if foreach:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` and `foreach` cannot be `True` together.\")\n            self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = True\n\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n                state = self.state[p]\n                state[\"step\"] = (\n                    torch.zeros(\n                        (),\n                        dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(is_fused=group[\"fused\"]),\n                        device=p.device,\n                    )\n                    if group[\"fused\"]\n                    else torch.tensor(0.0, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n                )\n                init_value = (\n                    complex(initial_accumulator_value, initial_accumulator_value)\n                    if torch.is_complex(p)\n                    else initial_accumulator_value\n                )\n                state[\"sum\"] = torch.full_like(\n                    p, init_value, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                )\n\n    def __setstate__(self, state):\n        super().__setstate__(state)\n        #  define \"fused\" for\n        #  MYPY error: Name \"fused\" may be undefined\n        fused = None\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n            fused = group.setdefault(\"fused\", None)\n\n        state_values = list(self.state.values())\n        step_is_tensor = (len(state_values) != 0) and torch.is_tensor(\n            state_values[0][\"step\"]\n        )\n        if not step_is_tensor:\n            for s in state_values:\n                s[\"step\"] = torch.tensor(\n                    float(s[\"step\"]), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(is_fused=fused)\n                )\n\n    def share_memory(self):\n        \"\"\"Calls tensor.share_memory_() on the state sum tensors.\"\"\"\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n                state = self.state[p]\n                state[\"sum\"].share_memory_()\n\n    def _init_group(self, group, params_with_grad, grads, state_sums, state_steps):\n        has_sparse_grad, has_complex = False, False\n        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n            if p.grad is not None:\n                if group[\"fused\"] and getattr(\n                    self,\n                    \"_need_device_dtype_check_for_fused\",\n                    True,\n                ):\n                    _device_dtype_check_for_fused(p, cuda_unsupported=True)\n                    self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = False\n                has_sparse_grad |= p.grad.is_sparse\n                has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n                params_with_grad.append(p)\n                grads.append(p.grad)\n                state = self.state[p]\n                state_sums.append(state[\"sum\"])\n                state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n\n        return has_sparse_grad, has_complex\n\n    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n    def step(self, closure=None):\n        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n\n        Args:\n            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n                and returns the loss.\n        \"\"\"\n        loss = None\n\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                loss = closure()\n\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n            state_sums: list[Tensor] = []\n            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n\n            has_sparse_grad, has_complex = self._init_group(\n                group, params_with_grad, grads, state_sums, state_steps\n            )\n\n            adagrad(\n                params_with_grad,\n                grads,\n                state_sums,\n                state_steps,\n                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n                lr_decay=group[\"lr_decay\"],\n                eps=group[\"eps\"],\n                has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n                fused=group[\"fused\"],\n                grad_scale=getattr(self, \"grad_scale\", None),\n                found_inf=getattr(self, \"found_inf\", None),\n            )\n\n        return loss\n\n\nAdagrad.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements Adagrad algorithm.\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n                \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{12mm}    \\tau \\text{ (initial accumulator value)}, \\: \\eta\\text{ (lr decay)}\\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} :  state\\_sum_0 \\leftarrow \\tau                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} \\tilde{\\gamma}    \\leftarrow \\gamma / (1 +(t-1) \\eta)                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} \\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda \\theta_{t-1}                             \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}state\\_sum_t  \\leftarrow  state\\_sum_{t-1} + g^2_t                      \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow\n                \\theta_{t-1}- \\tilde{\\gamma} \\frac{g_t}{\\sqrt{state\\_sum_t}+\\epsilon}            \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning\n    and Stochastic Optimization`_.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n        lr_decay (float, optional): learning rate decay (default: 0)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        initial_accumulator_value (float, optional): initial value of the\n            sum of squares of gradients (default: 0)\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-10)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        fused (bool, optional): whether the fused implementation (CPU only) is used.\n            Currently, `torch.float64`, `torch.float32`, `torch.float16`, and `torch.bfloat16`\n            are supported. (default: None). Please note that the fused implementations does not\n            support sparse or complex gradients.\n    .. _Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning and Stochastic\n        Optimization: http://jmlr.org/papers/v12/duchi11a.html\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting these as kwargs for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs Adagrad algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adagrad` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\n    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\n    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\n    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\n    if fused is None and foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n\n    if fused is None:\n        fused = False\n    if foreach is None:\n        foreach = False\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\")\n\n    if fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _fused_adagrad\n    elif foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_adagrad\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_adagrad\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        state_sums,\n        state_steps,\n        lr=lr,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        lr_decay=lr_decay,\n        eps=eps,\n        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n        grad_scale=grad_scale,\n        found_inf=found_inf,\n    )\n\n\ndef _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, values):\n    size = grad.size()\n    return torch.sparse_coo_tensor(grad_indices, values, size)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for param, grad, state_sum, step_t in zip(params, grads, state_sums, state_steps):\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n        step = _get_value(step_t)\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if grad.is_sparse:\n                raise RuntimeError(\n                    \"weight_decay option is not compatible with sparse gradients\"\n                )\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        clr = lr / (1 + (step - 1) * lr_decay)\n\n        if grad.is_sparse:\n            grad = grad.coalesce()  # the update is non-linear so indices must be unique\n            grad_indices = grad._indices()\n            grad_values = grad._values()\n\n            state_sum.add_(_make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values.pow(2)))\n            std = state_sum.sparse_mask(grad)\n            std_values = std._values().sqrt_().add_(eps)\n            param.add_(\n                _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values / std_values), alpha=-clr\n            )\n        else:\n            is_complex = torch.is_complex(param)\n            if is_complex:\n                grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n                state_sum = torch.view_as_real(state_sum)\n                param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n            state_sum.addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1)\n            if differentiable:\n                std = state_sum.sqrt() + eps\n            else:\n                std = state_sum.sqrt().add_(eps)\n            param.addcdiv_(grad, std, value=-clr)\n            if is_complex:\n                param = torch.view_as_complex(param)\n                state_sum = torch.view_as_complex(state_sum)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    # Foreach functions will throw errors if given empty lists\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensorlists = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_state_sums_,\n        device_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensorlists.values():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        if device_has_sparse_grad:\n            _single_tensor_adagrad(\n                device_params,\n                device_grads,\n                device_state_sums,\n                device_state_steps,\n                lr=lr,\n                weight_decay=weight_decay,\n                lr_decay=lr_decay,\n                eps=eps,\n                has_sparse_grad=True,\n                maximize=maximize,\n                differentiable=differentiable,\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n                grad_scale=grad_scale,\n                found_inf=found_inf,\n            )\n            continue\n\n        # Handle complex parameters\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(device_params, device_grads, device_state_sums)\n\n        if maximize:\n            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        minus_clr = [\n            -lr / (1 + (_get_value(step) - 1) * lr_decay) for step in device_state_steps\n        ]\n\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(device_state_sums, device_grads, device_grads, value=1)\n\n        std = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_state_sums)\n        torch._foreach_add_(std, eps)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0 or maximize:\n            # Again, reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated\n            torch._foreach_mul_(device_grads, minus_clr)\n            numerator = device_grads\n        else:\n            numerator = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, minus_clr)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        torch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, numerator, std)\n\n\ndef _fused_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n) -> None:\n    if not params:\n        return\n    if has_sparse_grad or has_complex:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support sparse grad or complex param\")\n\n    if differentiable:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"adagrad with fused=True does not support differentiable=True\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grad_scale_dict = (\n        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else None\n    )\n    found_inf_dict = {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else None\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (device, _), (\n        (\n            device_params_,\n            device_grads_,\n            device_state_sums_,\n            device_state_steps_,\n        ),\n        _,\n    ) in grouped_tensors.items():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device_grad_scale, device_found_inf = None, None\n        if grad_scale is not None and grad_scale_dict is not None:\n            if device not in grad_scale_dict:\n                grad_scale_dict[device] = grad_scale.to(device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[index]\n            device_grad_scale = grad_scale_dict[device]  # type: ignore[index]\n        if found_inf is not None and found_inf_dict is not None:\n            if found_inf not in found_inf_dict:\n                found_inf_dict[device] = found_inf.to(device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[index]\n            device_found_inf = found_inf_dict[device]  # type: ignore[index]\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n        torch._fused_adagrad_(\n            device_params,\n            device_grads,\n            device_state_sums,\n            device_state_steps,\n            lr=lr,\n            lr_decay=lr_decay,\n            weight_decay=weight_decay,\n            eps=eps,\n            maximize=maximize,\n            grad_scale=device_grad_scale,\n            found_inf=device_found_inf,\n        )\n        if device_found_inf is not None:\n            torch._foreach_sub_(\n                device_state_steps, [device_found_inf] * len(device_state_steps)\n            )\n",

Foreach functions will throw errors if given empty lists\n    if check_empty_params(params):\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensorlists = group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_state_sums_,\n        device_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensorlists.values():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        if device_has_sparse_grad:\n            _single_tensor_adagrad(\n                device_params,\n                device_grads,\n                device_state_sums,\n                device_state_steps,\n                lr=lr,\n                weight_decay=weight_decay,\n                lr_decay=lr_decay,\n                eps=eps,\n                has_sparse_grad=True,\n                maximize=maximize,\n                differentiable=differentiable,\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n                grad_scale=grad_scale,\n                found_inf=found_inf,\n            )\n            continue\n\n        # Handle complex parameters\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(device_params, device_grads, device_state_sums)\n\n        device_grads = apply_maximize(device_grads, maximize)\n\n        # Update steps\n        handle_step_updates(device_state_steps)\n\n        device_grads = apply_weight_decay(\n            device_grads, device_params, weight_decay, maximize\n        )",

The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )",
  "originalFile": "# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\nfrom typing import cast, Optional, Union\n\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\n\nfrom ._foreach import (\n    apply_maximize,\n    apply_weight_decay,\n    check_empty_params,\n    group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype,\n    handle_step_updates,\n)\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _device_dtype_check_for_fused,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    _get_value,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)\n\n\n__all__ = [\"Adagrad\", \"adagrad\"]\n\n\nclass Adagrad(Optimizer):\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        params: ParamsT,\n        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 1e-2,\n        lr_decay: float = 0,\n        weight_decay: float = 0,\n        initial_accumulator_value: float = 0,\n        eps: float = 1e-10,\n        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n        *,\n        maximize: bool = False,\n        differentiable: bool = False,\n        fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    ):\n        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= lr_decay:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid lr_decay value: {lr_decay}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= initial_accumulator_value:\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"Invalid initial_accumulator_value value: {initial_accumulator_value}\"\n            )\n        if not 0.0 <= eps:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid epsilon value: {eps}\")\n\n        defaults = {\n            \"lr\": lr,\n            \"lr_decay\": lr_decay,\n            \"eps\": eps,\n            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n            \"initial_accumulator_value\": initial_accumulator_value,\n            \"foreach\": foreach,\n            \"maximize\": maximize,\n            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n            \"fused\": fused,\n        }\n        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n\n        if fused:\n            if differentiable:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support `differentiable`\")\n            if foreach:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` and `foreach` cannot be `True` together.\")\n            self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = True\n\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n                state = self.state[p]\n                state[\"step\"] = (\n                    torch.zeros(\n                        (),\n                        dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(is_fused=group[\"fused\"]),\n                        device=p.device,\n                    )\n                    if group[\"fused\"]\n                    else torch.tensor(0.0, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n                )\n                init_value = (\n                    complex(initial_accumulator_value, initial_accumulator_value)\n                    if torch.is_complex(p)\n                    else initial_accumulator_value\n                )\n                state[\"sum\"] = torch.full_like(\n                    p, init_value, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                )\n\n    def __setstate__(self, state):\n        super().__setstate__(state)\n        #  define \"fused\" for\n        #  MYPY error: Name \"fused\" may be undefined\n        fused = None\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n            fused = group.setdefault(\"fused\", None)\n\n        state_values = list(self.state.values())\n        step_is_tensor = (len(state_values) != 0) and torch.is_tensor(\n            state_values[0][\"step\"]\n        )\n        if not step_is_tensor:\n            for s in state_values:\n                s[\"step\"] = torch.tensor(\n                    float(s[\"step\"]), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(is_fused=fused)\n                )\n\n    def share_memory(self):\n        \"\"\"Calls tensor.share_memory_() on the state sum tensors.\"\"\"\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n                state = self.state[p]\n                state[\"sum\"].share_memory_()\n\n    def _init_group(self, group, params_with_grad, grads, state_sums, state_steps):\n        has_sparse_grad, has_complex = False, False\n        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n            if p.grad is not None:\n                if group[\"fused\"] and getattr(\n                    self,\n                    \"_need_device_dtype_check_for_fused\",\n                    True,\n                ):\n                    _device_dtype_check_for_fused(p, cuda_unsupported=True)\n                    self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = False\n                has_sparse_grad |= p.grad.is_sparse\n                has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n                params_with_grad.append(p)\n                grads.append(p.grad)\n                state = self.state[p]\n                state_sums.append(state[\"sum\"])\n                state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n\n        return has_sparse_grad, has_complex\n\n    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n    def step(self, closure=None):\n        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n\n        Args:\n            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n                and returns the loss.\n        \"\"\"\n        loss = None\n\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                loss = closure()\n\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n            state_sums: list[Tensor] = []\n            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n\n            has_sparse_grad, has_complex = self._init_group(\n                group, params_with_grad, grads, state_sums, state_steps\n            )\n\n            adagrad(\n                params_with_grad,\n                grads,\n                state_sums,\n                state_steps,\n                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n                lr_decay=group[\"lr_decay\"],\n                eps=group[\"eps\"],\n                has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n                fused=group[\"fused\"],\n                grad_scale=getattr(self, \"grad_scale\", None),\n                found_inf=getattr(self, \"found_inf\", None),\n            )\n\n        return loss\n\n\nAdagrad.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements Adagrad algorithm.\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n                \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{12mm}    \\tau \\text{ (initial accumulator value)}, \\: \\eta\\text{ (lr decay)}\\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} :  state\\_sum_0 \\leftarrow \\tau                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} \\tilde{\\gamma}    \\leftarrow \\gamma / (1 +(t-1) \\eta)                  \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm} \\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda \\theta_{t-1}                             \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}state\\_sum_t  \\leftarrow  state\\_sum_{t-1} + g^2_t                      \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow\n                \\theta_{t-1}- \\tilde{\\gamma} \\frac{g_t}{\\sqrt{state\\_sum_t}+\\epsilon}            \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning\n    and Stochastic Optimization`_.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n        lr_decay (float, optional): learning rate decay (default: 0)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        initial_accumulator_value (float, optional): initial value of the\n            sum of squares of gradients (default: 0)\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-10)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        fused (bool, optional): whether the fused implementation (CPU only) is used.\n            Currently, `torch.float64`, `torch.float32`, `torch.float16`, and `torch.bfloat16`\n            are supported. (default: None). Please note that the fused implementations does not\n            support sparse or complex gradients.\n    .. _Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning and Stochastic\n        Optimization: http://jmlr.org/papers/v12/duchi11a.html\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting these as kwargs for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs Adagrad algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adagrad` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\n    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\n    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\n    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\n    if fused is None and foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n\n    if fused is None:\n        fused = False\n    if foreach is None:\n        foreach = False\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\")\n\n    if fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _fused_adagrad\n    elif foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_adagrad\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_adagrad\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        state_sums,\n        state_steps,\n        lr=lr,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        lr_decay=lr_decay,\n        eps=eps,\n        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n        grad_scale=grad_scale,\n        found_inf=found_inf,\n    )\n\n\ndef _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, values):\n    size = grad.size()\n    return torch.sparse_coo_tensor(grad_indices, values, size)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for param, grad, state_sum, step_t in zip(params, grads, state_sums, state_steps):\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n        step = _get_value(step_t)\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if grad.is_sparse:\n                raise RuntimeError(\n                    \"weight_decay option is not compatible with sparse gradients\"\n                )\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        clr = lr / (1 + (step - 1) * lr_decay)\n\n        if grad.is_sparse:\n            grad = grad.coalesce()  # the update is non-linear so indices must be unique\n            grad_indices = grad._indices()\n            grad_values = grad._values()\n\n            state_sum.add_(_make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values.pow(2)))\n            std = state_sum.sparse_mask(grad)\n            std_values = std._values().sqrt_().add_(eps)\n            param.add_(\n                _make_sparse(grad, grad_indices, grad_values / std_values), alpha=-clr\n            )\n        else:\n            is_complex = torch.is_complex(param)\n            if is_complex:\n                grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n                state_sum = torch.view_as_real(state_sum)\n                param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n            state_sum.addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1)\n            if differentiable:\n                std = state_sum.sqrt() + eps\n            else:\n                std = state_sum.sqrt().add_(eps)\n            param.addcdiv_(grad, std, value=-clr)\n            if is_complex:\n                param = torch.view_as_complex(param)\n                state_sum = torch.view_as_complex(state_sum)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    # Foreach functions will throw errors if given empty lists\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensorlists = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_state_sums_,\n        device_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensorlists.values():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        if device_has_sparse_grad:\n            _single_tensor_adagrad(\n                device_params,\n                device_grads,\n                device_state_sums,\n                device_state_steps,\n                lr=lr,\n                weight_decay=weight_decay,\n                lr_decay=lr_decay,\n                eps=eps,\n                has_sparse_grad=True,\n                maximize=maximize,\n                differentiable=differentiable,\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n                grad_scale=grad_scale,\n                found_inf=found_inf,\n            )\n            continue\n\n        # Handle complex parameters\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(device_params, device_grads, device_state_sums)\n\n        if maximize:\n            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        minus_clr = [\n            -lr / (1 + (_get_value(step) - 1) * lr_decay) for step in device_state_steps\n        ]\n\n        torch._foreach_addcmul_(device_state_sums, device_grads, device_grads, value=1)\n\n        std = torch._foreach_sqrt(device_state_sums)\n        torch._foreach_add_(std, eps)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0 or maximize:\n            # Again, reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated\n            torch._foreach_mul_(device_grads, minus_clr)\n            numerator = device_grads\n        else:\n            numerator = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, minus_clr)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        torch._foreach_addcdiv_(device_params, numerator, std)\n\n\ndef _fused_adagrad(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    state_sums: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    lr_decay: float,\n    eps: float,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n) -> None:\n    if not params:\n        return\n    if has_sparse_grad or has_complex:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support sparse grad or complex param\")\n\n    if differentiable:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"adagrad with fused=True does not support differentiable=True\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grad_scale_dict = (\n        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else None\n    )\n    found_inf_dict = {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else None\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, state_sums, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (device, _), (\n        (\n            device_params_,\n            device_grads_,\n            device_state_sums_,\n            device_state_steps_,\n        ),\n        _,\n    ) in grouped_tensors.items():\n        device_params = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_state_sums = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_sums_)\n        device_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], device_state_steps_)\n\n        device_grad_scale, device_found_inf = None, None\n        if grad_scale is not None and grad_scale_dict is not None:\n            if device not in grad_scale_dict:\n                grad_scale_dict[device] = grad_scale.to(device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[index]\n            device_grad_scale = grad_scale_dict[device]  # type: ignore[index]\n        if found_inf is not None and found_inf_dict is not None:\n            if found_inf not in found_inf_dict:\n                found_inf_dict[device] = found_inf.to(device, non_blocking=True)  # type: ignore[index]\n            device_found_inf = found_inf_dict[device]  # type: ignore[index]\n        torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\n        torch._fused_adagrad_(\n            device_params,\n            device_grads,\n            device_state_sums,\n            device_state_steps,\n            lr=lr,\n            lr_decay=lr_decay,\n            weight_decay=weight_decay,\n            eps=eps,\n            maximize=maximize,\n            grad_scale=device_grad_scale,\n            found_inf=device_found_inf,\n        )\n        if device_found_inf is not None:\n            torch._foreach_sub_(\n                device_state_steps, [device_found_inf] * len(device_state_steps)\n            )\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

{
  "file": {
    "content": "# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\nfrom typing import cast, Optional, Union\n\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\n\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _capturable_doc,\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    _get_value,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)\n\n\n__all__ = [\"Adamax\", \"adamax\"]\n\n\nclass Adamax(Optimizer):\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        params: ParamsT,\n        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 2e-3,\n        betas: tuple[float, float] = (0.9, 0.999),\n        eps: float = 1e-8,\n        weight_decay: float = 0,\n        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n        *,\n        maximize: bool = False,\n        differentiable: bool = False,\n        capturable: bool = False,\n    ):\n        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= eps:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid epsilon value: {eps}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= betas[0] < 1.0:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid beta parameter at index 0: {betas[0]}\")",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/optim/adamax.py",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 483
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "def _multi_tensor_adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n            supports_xla=False\n        )\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_infs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        grouped_params_,\n        grouped_grads_,\n        grouped_exp_avgs_,\n        grouped_exp_infs_,\n        grouped_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n        grouped_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avgs_)\n        grouped_exp_infs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_infs_)\n        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(\n                grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_infs\n            )\n\n        if maximize:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if maximize:\n                # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        # Update biased first moment estimate.\n        torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - beta1)\n\n        # Update the exponentially weighted infinity norm.\n        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_exp_infs, beta2)\n\n        # in this case, we need to introduce a copy of the grads\n        # since one has not been introduced previously\n        if not maximize and weight_decay == 0:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_abs(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_abs_(grouped_grads)\n\n        torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, eps)\n        torch._foreach_maximum_(grouped_exp_infs, grouped_grads)\n\n        bias_corrections: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        if capturable:\n            bias_corrections = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, grouped_state_steps)\n            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_corrections, 1)\n            torch._foreach_div_(bias_corrections, lr)\n\n            denom = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_exp_infs, bias_corrections)\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_exp_avgs, denom)\n        else:\n            bias_corrections = [\n                1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step) for step in grouped_state_steps\n            ]\n            step_size = [(_get_value(lr) / bc) * -1 for bc in bias_corrections]\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(\n                grouped_params, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_infs, step_size\n            )\n\n\n@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_adamax)\ndef adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    capturable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs adamax algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adamax` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and not all(\n        isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps\n    ):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    if foreach is None:",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/optim/adamax.py",
    "numLines": 150,

    "totalLines": 483

  "oldString": "from .optimizer import (\n    _capturable_doc,\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    _get_value,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)",
  "originalFile": "# mypy: allow-untyped-defs\nfrom typing import cast, Optional, Union\n\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\n\nfrom .optimizer import (\n    _capturable_doc,\n    _default_to_fused_or_foreach,\n    _differentiable_doc,\n    _disable_dynamo_if_unsupported,\n    _foreach_doc,\n    _get_capturable_supported_devices,\n    _get_scalar_dtype,\n    _get_value,\n    _maximize_doc,\n    _params_doc,\n    _to_scalar,\n    _use_grad_for_differentiable,\n    _view_as_real,\n    Optimizer,\n    ParamsT,\n)\n\n\n__all__ = [\"Adamax\", \"adamax\"]\n\n\nclass Adamax(Optimizer):\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        params: ParamsT,\n        lr: Union[float, Tensor] = 2e-3,\n        betas: tuple[float, float] = (0.9, 0.999),\n        eps: float = 1e-8,\n        weight_decay: float = 0,\n        foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n        *,\n        maximize: bool = False,\n        differentiable: bool = False,\n        capturable: bool = False,\n    ):\n        if isinstance(lr, Tensor) and lr.numel() != 1:\n            raise ValueError(\"Tensor lr must be 1-element\")\n        if not 0.0 <= lr:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid learning rate: {lr}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= eps:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid epsilon value: {eps}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= betas[0] < 1.0:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid beta parameter at index 0: {betas[0]}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= betas[1] < 1.0:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid beta parameter at index 1: {betas[1]}\")\n        if not 0.0 <= weight_decay:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid weight_decay value: {weight_decay}\")\n\n        defaults = {\n            \"lr\": lr,\n            \"betas\": betas,\n            \"eps\": eps,\n            \"weight_decay\": weight_decay,\n            \"foreach\": foreach,\n            \"maximize\": maximize,\n            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n            \"capturable\": capturable,\n        }\n        super().__init__(params, defaults)\n\n    def __setstate__(self, state):\n        super().__setstate__(state)\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"capturable\", False)\n            for p in group[\"params\"]:\n                p_state = self.state.get(p, [])\n                if len(p_state) != 0 and not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n                    step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n                    p_state[\"step\"] = (\n                        torch.tensor(\n                            step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n                        )\n                        if group[\"capturable\"]\n                        else torch.tensor(step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n                    )\n\n    def _init_group(\n        self, group, params_with_grad, grads, exp_avgs, exp_infs, state_steps\n    ):\n        has_complex = False\n        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n            if p.grad is None:\n                continue\n            has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n            params_with_grad.append(p)\n            if p.grad.is_sparse:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"Adamax does not support sparse gradients\")\n            grads.append(p.grad)\n\n            state = self.state[p]\n\n            # State initialization\n            if len(state) == 0:\n                state[\"step\"] = (\n                    torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n                    if group[\"capturable\"]\n                    else torch.tensor(0.0, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n                )\n                state[\"exp_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                    p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                )\n                state[\"exp_inf\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n                    p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n                )\n\n            exp_avgs.append(state[\"exp_avg\"])\n            exp_infs.append(state[\"exp_inf\"])\n            state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n\n        return has_complex\n\n    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n    def step(self, closure=None):\n        \"\"\"Performs a single optimization step.\n\n        Args:\n            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n                and returns the loss.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n\n        loss = None\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                loss = closure()\n\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n            exp_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n            exp_infs: list[Tensor] = []\n            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n\n            beta1, beta2 = group[\"betas\"]\n            eps = group[\"eps\"]\n            lr = group[\"lr\"]\n            weight_decay = group[\"weight_decay\"]\n            foreach = group[\"foreach\"]\n            maximize = group[\"maximize\"]\n            differentiable = group[\"differentiable\"]\n            capturable = group[\"capturable\"]\n\n            has_complex = self._init_group(\n                group, params_with_grad, grads, exp_avgs, exp_infs, state_steps\n            )\n\n            adamax(\n                params_with_grad,\n                grads,\n                exp_avgs,\n                exp_infs,\n                state_steps,\n                eps=eps,\n                beta1=beta1,\n                beta2=beta2,\n                lr=lr,\n                weight_decay=weight_decay,\n                foreach=foreach,\n                maximize=maximize,\n                differentiable=differentiable,\n                capturable=capturable,\n                has_complex=has_complex,\n            )\n\n        return loss\n\n\nAdamax.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements Adamax algorithm (a variant of Adam based on infinity norm).\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n                \\text{ (betas)},\\theta_0 \\text{ (params)},f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)},\n                \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                                                \\\\\n            &\\hspace{13mm}    \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                                          \\\\\n            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n                u_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( infinity norm)}                                 \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                                    \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}m_t      \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t               \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}u_t      \\leftarrow   \\mathrm{max}(\\beta_2 u_{t-1}, |g_{t}|+\\epsilon)   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\frac{\\gamma m_t}{(1-\\beta^t_1) u_t} \\\\\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization`_.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 2e-3)\n        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n            running averages of gradient and its square\n        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        {_capturable_doc}\n\n    .. _Adam\\: A Method for Stochastic Optimization:\n        https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.6980\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n        grad = grads[i]\n        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n        exp_inf = exp_infs[i]\n        step_t = state_steps[i]\n\n        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n            assert (\n                param.device.type == step_t.device.type\n                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            ), (\n                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n            )\n\n        # update step\n        step_t += 1\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        if torch.is_complex(param):\n            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n            exp_inf = torch.view_as_real(exp_inf)\n\n        # Update biased first moment estimate.\n        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - beta1)\n        # Update the exponentially weighted infinity norm.\n        if not differentiable:\n            torch.maximum(\n                exp_inf.mul_(beta2),\n                grad.abs().add_(eps),\n                out=exp_inf,\n            )\n        else:\n            norm_buf = torch.cat(\n                [exp_inf.mul_(beta2).unsqueeze(0), grad.abs().add_(eps).unsqueeze_(0)],\n                0,\n            )\n            exp_inf.copy_(torch.amax(norm_buf, 0, keepdim=False))\n\n        if capturable:\n            # why jump through extra hoops and negate bias_correction? check out #121238\n            # once fixed, we should use bias_correction with addcdiv value=-1 for readability\n            neg_bias_correction = beta1**step_t - 1\n            neg_bias_correction.div_(lr)\n            denom = exp_inf * neg_bias_correction\n            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom)\n        else:\n            bias_correction = 1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step_t)\n            clr = lr / bias_correction\n\n            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, exp_inf, value=-clr)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n            supports_xla=False\n        )\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_infs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        grouped_params_,\n        grouped_grads_,\n        grouped_exp_avgs_,\n        grouped_exp_infs_,\n        grouped_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n        grouped_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avgs_)\n        grouped_exp_infs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_infs_)\n        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(\n                grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_infs\n            )\n\n        if maximize:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if maximize:\n                # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        # Update biased first moment estimate.\n        torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - beta1)\n\n        # Update the exponentially weighted infinity norm.\n        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_exp_infs, beta2)\n\n        # in this case, we need to introduce a copy of the grads\n        # since one has not been introduced previously\n        if not maximize and weight_decay == 0:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_abs(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_abs_(grouped_grads)\n\n        torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, eps)\n        torch._foreach_maximum_(grouped_exp_infs, grouped_grads)\n\n        bias_corrections: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n        if capturable:\n            bias_corrections = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, grouped_state_steps)\n            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_corrections, 1)\n            torch._foreach_div_(bias_corrections, lr)\n\n            denom = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_exp_infs, bias_corrections)\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, grouped_exp_avgs, denom)\n        else:\n            bias_corrections = [\n                1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step) for step in grouped_state_steps\n            ]\n            step_size = [(_get_value(lr) / bc) * -1 for bc in bias_corrections]\n            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(\n                grouped_params, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_infs, step_size\n            )\n\n\n@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_adamax)\ndef adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    maximize: bool = False,\n    differentiable: bool = False,\n    capturable: bool = False,\n    has_complex: bool = False,\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs adamax algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.Adamax` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and not all(\n        isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps\n    ):\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n        )\n\n    if foreach is None:\n        _, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n            params, differentiable, use_fused=False\n        )\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n\n    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_adamax\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_adamax\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        grads,\n        exp_avgs,\n        exp_infs,\n        state_steps,\n        eps=eps,\n        beta1=beta1,\n        beta2=beta2,\n        lr=lr,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        differentiable=differentiable,\n        has_complex=has_complex,\n        capturable=capturable,\n    )\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/optim/adamax.py",
  "new_string": "def _multi_tensor_adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    if check_empty_params(params):\n        return\n\n    handle_capturable_devices(params, state_steps, capturable, supports_xla=False)\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_infs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        grouped_params_,\n        grouped_grads_,\n        grouped_exp_avgs_,\n        grouped_exp_infs_,\n        grouped_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n        grouped_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avgs_)\n        grouped_exp_infs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_infs_)\n        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(\n                grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_infs\n            )\n\n        grouped_grads = apply_maximize(grouped_grads, maximize)\n\n        # Update steps\n        handle_step_updates(grouped_state_steps)\n\n        grouped_grads = apply_weight_decay(\n            grouped_grads, grouped_params, weight_decay, maximize\n        )",
  "old_string": "def _multi_tensor_adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n            supports_xla=False\n        )\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_infs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n    )\n    for (\n        grouped_params_,\n        grouped_grads_,\n        grouped_exp_avgs_,\n        grouped_exp_infs_,\n        grouped_state_steps_,\n    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n        grouped_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avgs_)\n        grouped_exp_infs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_infs_)\n        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(\n                grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_infs\n            )\n\n        if maximize:\n            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        # Update steps\n        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            if maximize:\n                # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )",
  "replace_all": false
}

  "oldString": "def _multi_tensor_adamax(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n    exp_infs: list[Tensor],\n    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    *,\n    eps: float,\n    beta1: float,\n    beta2: float,\n    lr: float,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    maximize: bool,\n    differentiable: bool,\n    capturable: bool,\n    has_complex: bool,\n):\n    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n            supports_xla=False\n        )\n        assert all(\n            p.device.type == step.device.type\n            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n        ), (\n            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n        )\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_infs, state_steps]  #
